  • MPSTensor.NonzeroProportionalMPV₂.eventually
  • MPSTensor.NonzeroProportionalMPV₂.symm
  • MPSTensor.SameMPV₂.toNonzeroProportionalMPV₂
Lemma 7.12Norm of embedded matrix

\(\| \mathrm{vec}(X)\| ^2 = \| X\| _F^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.13Restricted normal-CF-BNT hypotheses give a BNT

If a family is in normal canonical form with BNT separation and each block is also normal in the algebraic sense, then the blocks form a basis of normal tensors for the associated block-diagonal tensor. The blocks here are distinct representatives.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.252Normalized boundary matrix identities

Let \((A_a)_{a\in I}\), \((C_a)_{a\in I}\), and \((D_b)_{b\in I}\) be matrices in \(M_D(\mathbb {C})\), indexed by a finite alphabet. If

\[ \sum _a A_a A_a^\dagger =\mathbb {1}, \qquad A_b C_a=D_b A_a \]

for all \(a,b\in I\), and if

\[ E=\sum _a C_a A_a^\dagger , \]

then

\[ D_b=A_bE, \qquad A_bC_a=A_bEA_a \]

for all \(a,b\in I\).

In particular, the same calculation applies when the alphabet is the finite set of words of a fixed length and \(A_\beta \) denotes \(A_{\beta _1}\cdots A_{\beta _K}\).

The same argument also applies when the normalized family is indexed by a finite set \(J\), while \((A_b)_{b\in I}\) and \((D_b)_{b\in I}\) are indexed by a set \(I\). If \((B_a)_{a\in J}\) and \((C_a)_{a\in J}\) are indexed by \(J\), and if for all \(a\in J\) and \(b\in I\),

\[ \sum _a B_aB_a^\dagger =\mathbb {1}, \qquad A_bC_a=D_bB_a \]

then with

\[ E=\sum _a C_aB_a^\dagger \]

one has

\[ D_b=A_bE, \qquad A_bC_a=A_bEB_a. \]

This includes the case where \(I\) and \(J\) are word sets of different lengths.

LaTeX

Lemma 7.87Normalized peripheral unitary

Peripheral unitary eigenvectors can be normalized compatibly with the faithful fixed-point state.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.41String order prevents decay to zero

If \(A\) has string order for \(u\) with respect to a boundary state \(\Lambda \), then for some boundary matrices \(X,Y\) the sequence \(R_L(u;X,Y)\) does not tend to \(0\) as \(L \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.6Ambient-space dimension

The above coefficient space satisfies

\[ \dim \! (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C})=d^L. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.30One-site injectivity gives one-block injectivity

If the one-site matrices \(\{ A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) span \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), then the length-one word products span \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), so \(A\) is \(1\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.82One-sided boundary matrices are unique

Let \(A\) be \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\). If two boundary matrices have the same product with every one-site tensor on one fixed side, then the two boundary matrices are equal.

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.right_witness_unique_of_isNBlkInjective
  • MPSTensor.left_witness_unique_of_isNBlkInjective
Lemma 8.32Normality and Kraus rank under one-step augmentation

Let \(X \in S_1(A)\), and let \(\widetilde A\) be the one-step augmentation of \(A\) by \(X\). If \(A\) is normal, then \(\widetilde A\) is normal, and

\[ \operatorname{kr}(\widetilde A)=\operatorname{kr}(A). \]

Lemma 8.31Redundant one-step augmentation

Let \(X \in S_1(A)\), and let \(\widetilde A\) be the one-step augmentation of \(A\) by \(X\). Then

\[ S_n(\widetilde A)=S_n(A) \]

for every \(n \ge 0\).

Lemma 13.47Open-chain iteration for a sequence of subspaces

Let \(S_m\subseteq (\mathbb {C}^d)^{\otimes m}\) be subspaces, and let \(0{\lt}L\le N\). Suppose that every non-wrapping length-\(L\) interval of \(\psi \), for every fixed choice of the complementary sites, lies in \(S_L\). Suppose also that, for every \(m\ge L\),

\[ \mathbb {C}^d\otimes S_m\cap S_m\otimes \mathbb {C}^d=S_{m+1}. \]

Then \(\psi \in S_N\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.13Positive matrix-valued integrals

Let \(f\) be a function from a measure space to a finite matrix algebra. If \(f(x)\ge 0\) for almost every \(x\), then \(\int f(x)\, d\mu (x)\ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.6Compression auxiliary for Jensen

Let \(Y\) be positive definite and let \(W\) be an isometry. Then

\begin{equation} \label{eq:opconv_inverse_compression_le} (W^\ast Y W)^{-1} \le W^\ast Y^{-1} W. \end{equation}
1

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.10Positive-map resolvent inequality

Let \(T\) be a positive subunital map, let \(A\ge 0\), and let \(t{\gt}0\). Then

\[ (TA+t\mathbb {1})^{-1} \le T((A+t\mathbb {1})^{-1})+t^{-1}(\mathbb {1}-T\mathbb {1}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.11Positive-map real-power integrand inequality

Let \(T\) be a positive subunital map, let \(A\ge 0\), let \(p\in (0,1)\), and let \(t{\gt}0\). Then

\[ T\! \left(f_{p,t}(A)\right) \le f_{p,t}(TA), \qquad f_{p,t}(x)=t^p\left(t^{-1}-(t+x)^{-1}\right). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.12Positive-map convex real-power integrand inequality

Let \(T\) be a positive subunital map, let \(A\ge 0\), let \(p\in (1,2)\), and let \(t{\gt}0\). Then

\[ g_{p,t}(TA) \le T\! \left(g_{p,t}(A)\right), \qquad g_{p,t}(x)=t^{p-2}x+t^p(t+x)^{-1}-t^{p-1}. \]

The inequality is reversed relative to the concave integrand, since \(g_{p,t}\) is operator convex.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.7Finite-POVM compression identities

If the defect block satisfies

\begin{equation} \label{eq:opconv_defect_block_relation} S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}- \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast , \end{equation}
2

then the dilation is an isometry, compressing the weighted scalar block-diagonal matrix gives

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } w_i\, C_i C_i^\ast + t\, S S^\ast , \]

the defect relation rewrites as

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast + S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}, \]

and strict positivity of all weights together with \(t{\gt}0\) implies positive definiteness of the scalar block-diagonal matrix. If all weights are nonzero and the defect scalar is nonzero, the inverse diagonal has reciprocal weights, and compressing this inverse gives

\[ \sum _{i \in \iota } w_i^{-1}\, C_i C_i^\ast + t^{-1}\, S S^\ast . \]
LaTeX

  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povmIsometry_compress_diagonal
  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povm_sum_add_defect

  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povmDiagonal_inv
  • TNLean.OperatorJensen.povmIsometry_compress_diagonal_inv
Lemma 18.8Finite-POVM resolvent inequality

Let \(w_i\ge 0\) and \(t{\gt}0\). If the defect block satisfies

\[ S S^\ast = \mathbb {1}- \sum _{i \in \iota } C_i C_i^\ast , \]

then

\begin{equation} \label{eq:opconv_povm_resolvent_bound} \left(\sum _{i \in \iota } w_i\, C_i C_i^\ast + t\, \mathbb {1}\right)^{-1} \le \sum _{i \in \iota } (w_i+t)^{-1}\, C_i C_i^\ast + t^{-1}\, S S^\ast . \end{equation}
3

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.9Spectral finite-POVM resolution

Let \(A\ge 0\) be a finite matrix. There are positive semidefinite matrices \(P_i\) and nonnegative real numbers \(\lambda _i\) such that

\[ \sum _i P_i=\mathbb {1}, \qquad A=\sum _i \lambda _i P_i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.188Ordered projection row-sum quadratic-form reduction

Let \(P_i\) be a finite family of symmetric projections and let \(H=\sum _i P_i\). If the ordered off-diagonal forms obey

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle P_i v, P_j v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )c_{ij}\operatorname {Re}\langle P_i v,v\rangle \qquad (i\ne j), \]

with row sums \(\sum _{j\ne i} c_{ij}\le 1\) and \(\gamma \le 1\), then \(H^2\ge \gamma H\) as a quadratic form.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.98Outside labels determine boundary-restriction matrices

Let \(A\) be \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(L_0\le M\). If an outside configuration \(\rho \) has the same word \(\mu \) as \(\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )\) on the sites outside the last-site cyclic window, then the two matrices representing that restriction agree:

\[ Y_\rho =Y_{\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )}. \]

The same assertion holds for the second boundary-crossing window: if \(\rho \) has the same outside word as \(\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )\), then

\[ Y_\rho =Y_{\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )}. \]

This is the boundary-matrix independence step used to choose one representative outside configuration for each outside word in the closure-property matrix comparison.

LaTeX

Lemma 2.49Overlap equals conjugate inner product

\(O_{AB}(N) = \overline{\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle }\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.50Overlap determined by positive-length MPV

If \(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(A')_\sigma \) and \(V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B')_\sigma \) for all \(N {\gt} 0\) and all \(\sigma \), then \(O_{AB}(N) = O_{A'B'}(N)\) for all positive \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.29Mixed-transfer spectral radius from unit-modulus overlap

Let \(A\) and \(B\) have the same bond dimension. If \(\| \langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \| \to 1\), then the mixed transfer map has spectral radius at least \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.81Padded complement annihilation

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective, \(q \ge 1\), and \(k \le qL_0\), then the two one-sided annihilation statements are:

\[ Z A^w = 0 \quad (|w|=k) \qquad \Longrightarrow \qquad Z=0. \]

The left-handed form is

\[ A^w Z = 0 \quad (|w|=k) \qquad \Longrightarrow \qquad Z=0. \]
LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.eq_zero_of_mul_evalWord_eq_zero_of_isNBlkInjective_of_le_mul
  • MPSTensor.eq_zero_of_evalWord_mul_eq_zero_of_isNBlkInjective_of_le_mul
Lemma 13.239Pair algebra projections are full

If one of the two blocks is injective, then the corresponding coordinate projection of the pair algebra generated by the simultaneous one-letter pairs is the full matrix algebra. Every finite pair word lies in this pair algebra, and the all-word linear span is the submodule generated by the pair algebra. If the pair algebra is the graph of an algebra automorphism of the matrix algebra, Skolem–Noether turns this graph case into gauge-phase equivalence, so under non-gauge-equivalence the graph branch is impossible. For two blocks of unequal bond dimensions, the graph branch is also impossible because it would identify matrix algebras with different finite dimensions over \(\mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX

Lemma 13.240Conditional padding from exact homogeneous identity witnesses

Homogeneous identity-padding witnesses multiply by concatenation of words. Therefore, if one has a positive period and a full residue window of homogeneous pair spans, then the simultaneous identity pair lies in all sufficiently large homogeneous pair spans. Combined with all-length pair trace separation, this period-window input gives one homogeneous pair trace-separation length. This does not prove the block-separation span condition of [ PGVWC07 ] by itself: fullness of spans up to a bounded length is weaker than the exact homogeneous input used here.

LaTeX

Lemma 13.241Pair trace separation gives pairwise block separation

At a fixed length \(S\), the pair trace-separation criterion is dual to full pair product-span. Therefore a pair trace-separation witness supplies a linear combination of word evaluations that is identity on the first block and zero on the second. A common witness for all ordered distinct pairs gives pairwise block-separating words at the same length.

Lemma 10.23Sector decomposition formula

For a sector decomposition \(S\) with associated tensor \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\), the MPV satisfies

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g} c_{S,j}^{(N)} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.21Parent Hamiltonian annihilates the periodic MPS vector

For every \(N\) with \(L\le N\), the periodic MPS vector satisfies

\[ H_N(A,L)\, V^{(N)}(A) = 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.195Parent-Hamiltonian gap from the anticommutator martingale condition

Assume uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the Hilbert-space local terms satisfy the anticommutator martingale estimate

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -c_{ij}\Bigl(1-\frac{1}{4L}\Bigr) \bigl( \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \bigr) \]

for \(i\ne j\), with row and column coefficient sums bounded by one. If \(L{\gt}1\), then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\) and, for every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \frac{1}{4L}\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_quadratic_form_of_anticommutator_local_term_bounds
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_anticommutator_local_term_bounds
Lemma 13.208Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a cyclic anticommutator estimate

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and let \(i\sim _L j\) mean that the cyclic supports of the length-\(L\) windows at \(i\) and \(j\) meet. Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that overlapping off-diagonal terms satisfy

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -\Bigl(1-\frac{1}{4L}\Bigr)\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \bigl( \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \bigr). \]

Then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.210Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a cyclic overlap compression constant

Fix \(L{\gt}1\), and let \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair that

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

If \(\gamma {\gt}0\), \(\gamma \le 1\), and \(\eta \le (1-\gamma )/m\), then \(0{\lt}\gamma \) and, for every admissible \(N\) and every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

In particular, if \(\eta \ge 0\) and \(\eta m{\lt}1\), then \(0{\lt}1-\eta m\) and, for every admissible \(N\) and every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ (1-\eta m)\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

This strict compression form is a sufficient strengthening of the anticommutator martingale estimate in Cirac–Perez-Garcia–Schuch–Verstraete  [ CPGSV21 , Section IV.C ] . It does not prove the remaining MPS-specific estimate; it isolates the finite-row martingale argument once such a strengthening is supplied.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_norm_bound_of_le
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_norm_bound_of_lt
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_principal_angle_compression
Lemma 13.209Parent-Hamiltonian gap from sufficient cyclic norm compression

Fix \(L{\gt}1\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair that

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le (1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.211Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a cyclic operator-product norm constant

Fix \(L{\gt}1\), and let \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair that

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\| , \]

that is, a bound \(\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\| \le \eta \) on the operator-product norm. If \(\gamma {\gt}0\), \(\gamma \le 1\), and \(\eta \le (1-\gamma )/m\), then \(0{\lt}\gamma \) and, for every admissible \(N\) and every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

In particular, if \(\eta \ge 0\) and \(\eta m{\lt}1\), then \(0{\lt}1-\eta m\) and the same norm bound holds with \(\gamma =1-\eta m\).

This is the symmetric (operator-product) counterpart of Lemma 13.210, obtained from the anticommutator conversion recorded in Remark 13.187. Like that lemma it is a conditional reduction: the excitation projections satisfy \(\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\| =1\) whenever the overlapping ranges share a common nonzero vector, so the hypothesis is unsatisfiable for \(\eta {\lt}1\) in that case. It isolates the finite-row martingale argument once such a bound is supplied; the gap with the cited principal-angle estimates is documented in the paper-gap note docs/paper-gaps/cpgsv21_martingale_overlap.tex.

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_operator_norm_of_le
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_cyclic_window_overlap_operator_norm_of_lt
Lemma 13.207Parent-Hamiltonian gap from an overlapping cyclic martingale estimate

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and let \(i\sim _L j\) mean that the cyclic supports of the length-\(L\) windows at \(i\) and \(j\) meet. Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that overlapping off-diagonal terms satisfy

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_cyclic_overlap_ordered_cross_term} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle . \end{equation}
20

Then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.206Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a cyclic-window martingale estimate

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and let \(i\sim _L j\) mean that the cyclic supports of the length-\(L\) windows at \(i\) and \(j\) meet. Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that overlapping off-diagonal terms satisfy

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_cyclic_window_overlap_estimate} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\tfrac {1}{4L})\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle . \end{equation}
19

Then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\), and the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.186Parent-Hamiltonian quadratic-form reduction

Let \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) be the Hilbert-space representative of the parent Hamiltonian. If a constant \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) satisfies the uniform quadratic-form estimate

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_uniform_quadratic_form} \gamma \, \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v, v\rangle \le \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v, H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\rangle \end{equation}
16

for every \(N \ge 2L\) and every vector \(v\), then

\[ \gamma \, \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| \]

for every \(v \in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \). In particular, with \(L{\gt}1\) and \(\gamma = 1/(4L)\), the explicit gap-bound conclusion follows once this same quadratic-form estimate (??) is supplied.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_norm_bound_of_quadratic_form
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_quadratic_form
Lemma 13.22Frustration-freeness of the periodic MPS vector

The periodic MPS vector is a frustration-free ground state: for every site \(i\),

\[ h_i V^{(N)}(A)=0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.196Parent-Hamiltonian finite-overlap anticommutator reduction

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and set \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the overlap relation on local windows has at most \(m\) off-diagonal neighbours in each row and in each column. If non-overlapping local terms have non-negative anticommutator form, and overlapping local terms satisfy

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -\Bigl(1-\frac{1}{4L}\Bigr)\frac{1}{m} \bigl( \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle +\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \bigr), \]

then the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_quadratic_form_of_finite_overlap_anticommutator
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_gap_bound_of_finite_overlap_anticommutator
Lemma 13.198Finite-overlap norm-compression reduction

For a fixed chain length \(N\), let \(m{\gt}0\) and \(\gamma \le 1\). Suppose an overlap predicate on local windows has at most \(m\) overlapping off-diagonal windows in each row, non-overlapping local terms have non-negative ordered cross terms, and overlapping local terms satisfy

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le (1-\gamma )\frac{1}{m}\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}{}^2\ge \gamma H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}\) as a quadratic form. The same conclusion holds from a separate coefficient \(\eta \) satisfying \(\eta \le (1-\gamma )/m\).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_quadratic_form_of_finite_overlap_norm_bound_of_le
Lemma 13.205Parent-Hamiltonian gap from a finite-overlap martingale estimate

Fix \(L{\gt}1\) and set \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the hypotheses of the fixed-chain finite-overlap martingale reduction hold with \(\gamma =1/(4L)\) and this value of \(m\). Then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\), and the explicit norm lower bound with constant \(1/(4L)\) follows for vectors in \((\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.197Parent-Hamiltonian finite-overlap martingale quadratic reduction

For a fixed chain length \(N\), let \(m\) be a positive integer and let \(\gamma \le 1\). Suppose an overlap predicate on local windows has at most \(m\) overlapping off-diagonal windows in each row. If non-overlapping local terms have non-negative ordered cross terms, and overlapping local terms satisfy the one-sided ordered cross-term estimate

\[ \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )\frac{1}{m} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle , \]

and the Hilbert-space local terms are symmetric projections, then the Hilbert-space Hamiltonian satisfies \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}{}^2\ge \gamma H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}\) as a quadratic form.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.222Block-diagonal periodic-boundary ground space

By definition, for \(B=\bigoplus _{j=1}^g \mu _j A_j\) one has

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}(B) = \mathcal G_{N,L}\! \left(\bigoplus _{j=1}^g \mu _j A_j\right). \]
LaTeX
Lemma 13.194Parent-Hamiltonian gap from ordered local row bounds

Assume uniformly for every \(N\ge 2L\) that the Hilbert-space local terms satisfy the ordered row-sum estimate (??) with \(\gamma =1/(4L)\). If \(L{\gt}1\), then \(1/(4L){\gt}0\) and, for every \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \frac{1}{4L}\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.193Quadratic-form estimate from the martingale condition

For a fixed chain length \(N\), suppose the Hilbert-space local terms \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) satisfy the ordered row-sum estimate

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_ordered_rowsum_estimate} \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}} v,h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \ge -(1-\gamma )c_{ij}\operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \qquad (i\ne j), \end{equation}
18

with \(\sum _{j\ne i}c_{ij}\le 1\) and \(\gamma \le 1\). Then the Hilbert-space parent Hamiltonian satisfies

\[ \gamma \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v,v\rangle \le \operatorname {Re}\langle H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v, H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle \]

for every vector \(v\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.11Parent interaction annihilates ground-space elements

For any \(v \in G_L(A)\), we have \(h_L(A)\, v = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.177Kernel of the Hilbert-space parent interaction

The Hilbert-space local interaction \(P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) annihilates exactly the Hilbert-space local ground space:

\[ P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v=0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad v\in G_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.264Period-window form of the block intersection identity

Let \(p{\gt}0\). Suppose the length-\(p\) simultaneous block-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\), and suppose that for every \(0\le r{\lt}p\), the length-\(s+r\) simultaneous block-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\). Assume also the normalization

\[ \sum _a A^j_aA^{j\, \dagger }_a=I \]

for every block \(j\). Then there is a length \(N\) such that, for every \(n\ge N\), if

\[ S_n:=\bigvee _jG_{n+1}(A^j), \]

then

\[ \left\{ \psi :\forall b,\ \psi (-,b)\in S_n\right\} \cap \left\{ \psi :\forall a,\ \psi (a,-)\in S_n\right\} = \bigvee _jG_{n+2}(A^j). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.244Period-window propagation for blockwise product spans

Let \(p{\gt}0\). Suppose the length-\(p\) simultaneous block-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\), and suppose that for every \(0\le r{\lt}p\), the length-\(s+r\) simultaneous block-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\). Then there is a length \(N\) such that, for every \(n\ge N\), the length-\(n\) simultaneous block-word tuples span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\). More concretely, one may take \(N=s\): writing

\[ n=s+r+qp,\qquad 0\le r{\lt}p, \]

the span at length \(s+r\) propagates to the span at length \(n\) by multiplying \(q\) times by the period-\(p\) span.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.wordTupleSpanTop_add_mul_of_wordTupleSpanTop
  • MPSTensor.wordTupleSpanTop_eventually_of_wordTupleSpanTop_period_window
Lemma 13.48Periodic constraints and propagated subspaces

Let \(S_m\subseteq (\mathbb {C}^d)^{\otimes m}\) be subspaces, and let \(0{\lt}L\le N\). Suppose that \(G_L(A)\subseteq S_L\). Suppose also that, for every \(m\ge L\),

\[ \mathbb {C}^d\otimes S_m\cap S_m\otimes \mathbb {C}^d=S_{m+1}. \]

Then the periodic local constraints imply

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}(A)\subseteq S_N. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.9Equivalence structures for periodic relations

Both periodic relations are equivalence relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive).

Lemma 4.68Peripheral closure via adjoint fixed point

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a Kraus map that is unital (\(\sum _i K_i K_i^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\)), and assume:

  1. the adjoint Kraus map \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\), and

  2. \(E\) is irreducible.

Then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) is closed under powers: if \(\mu \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) then \(\mu ^n \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) for every \(n \in \mathbb {N}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.73Peripheral eigenvalues: product closure

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. If \(\mu , \nu \) are peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\), then so is \(\mu \nu \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 4.57Powering collapses peripheral eigenvalues

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a linear map, and assume that \(E\) has a nonzero fixed point \(\rho \neq 0\). Let \(p {\gt} 0\). If every peripheral eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(E\) satisfies \(\mu ^p = 1\), then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E^p) = \{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.85Peripheral unitary eigenvector

For an irreducible unital Schwarz map with faithful adjoint fixed point, each peripheral eigenvalue admits a unitary eigenvector.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.86Powers on a peripheral-unitary orbit

If \(E(U)=\mu U\) with \(|\mu |=1\) and \(U\) unitary, then \(E(U^n)=\mu ^n U^n\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.33Composition of permutation twists

If \(\sigma (gh) = \sigma (g) \circ \sigma (h)\) for all \(g, h \in G\), then

\[ A^{\sigma _{gh}} = (A^{\sigma _g})^{\sigma _h}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.32Identity permutation twist

If \(\sigma (1) = \operatorname{id}\), then \((A^{\sigma _1})^i = A^i\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.260Trace decompositions from fixed-boundary restrictions

Let \(A^1,\ldots ,A^g\) be block tensors. If \(\psi \in (\mathbb {C}^d)^{\otimes (n+2)}\) satisfies

\[ \psi (a,-)\in \bigvee _jG_{n+1}(A^j), \qquad \psi (-,b)\in \bigvee _jG_{n+1}(A^j) \]

for every physical index \(a,b\), then there are matrices \(C^j_a\) and \(D^j_b\) such that

\[ \psi =\sum _j\alpha _j, \qquad \alpha _j(i_1,\ldots ,i_{n+2}) = \operatorname{tr}\! \left( A^j_{i_{n+2}}C^j_{i_1} A^j_{i_2}\cdots A^j_{i_{n+1}} \right), \]

and, for every middle word \(w\) of length \(n\),

\[ \sum _j\operatorname{tr}\! \left(A^j_bC^j_aA^j_w\right) = \sum _j\operatorname{tr}\! \left(D^j_bA^j_aA^j_w\right). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.253Explicit \(C^j,D^j,E^j\) identities from trace decompositions

Let \(A^1,\ldots ,A^g\) be block tensors with the common word-span property at length \(m\). Fix matrices \(X_j\), and put

\[ D^j_\beta =X_jA^j_\beta . \]

Suppose that \(C^j_\rho \) is indexed by a block \(j\) and a complementary word \(\rho \) of length \(M\), and assume that, for every wrapped word \(\beta \) of length \(K\), every complementary word \(\rho \), and every middle word \(w\) of length \(m\),

\[ \sum _j\operatorname{tr}\! \left(A^j_\beta C^j_\rho A^j_w\right) = \sum _j\operatorname{tr}\! \left(D^j_\beta A^j_\rho A^j_w\right). \]

If each block satisfies

\[ \sum _a A^j_aA^{j\, \dagger }_a=I, \]

then for every block \(j\) there is a matrix

\[ E^j=\sum _\rho C^j_\rho \left(A^j_\rho \right)^\dagger \]

such that, for every wrapped word \(\beta \) and complementary word \(\rho \),

\[ D^j_\beta =A^j_\beta E^j, \qquad A^j_\beta C^j_\rho =A^j_\beta E^jA^j_\rho . \]

This is the calculation in [ PGVWC07 , Theorem 12, proof lines 1446–1451 ] , with the adjoint on \(A^j_\rho \) required by the displayed normalization.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.84Positive-definite trace lower bound

Let \(D\geq 1\), let \(M\in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be positive-definite, and let \(\lambda _{\min }(M)\) be its smallest Hermitian eigenvalue. For every \(X\in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ \lambda _{\min }(M)\, \operatorname{tr}(X^{\dagger }X) \leq \operatorname{tr}(XMX^{\dagger }). \]
LaTeX

Lemma 5.84Positive-definite trace test

If \(A {\gt} 0\), \(X \ge 0\), and \(\operatorname{tr}(A X) = 0\), then \(X = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.39Prepared collapsed BNT total dimension

For prepared trace-preserving primitive irreducible NTs of positive bond dimension with all copy weights nonzero, grouping CF summands by BNT representative preserves the total bond dimension: the collapsed sector decomposition has total bond dimension equal to the sum of the original block dimensions.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.38Prepared BNT representative classes preserve bond dimension

In a prepared family of positive-bond-dimension trace-preserving primitive irreducible NTs, every CF summand assigned to a BNT representative has that representative’s bond dimension.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.37Prepared phase-equivalent NTs have the same bond dimension

Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be trace-preserving, primitive, irreducible NTs of positive bond dimension. If their MPV families differ by a nonzero scalar power, then \(X\) and \(Y\) have the same bond dimension.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.61Primitive MPS tensors have nonzero word products

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then for every \(n \ge 0\) there exists a word \(w\) of length exactly \(n\) with \(A^w \neq 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.66Primitive tensors with positive-definite fixed point are irreducible

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with PSD fixed point \(\rho \) and \(\rho \) is positive definite, then \(A\) is an irreducible tensor. This is part of  [ SPGWC10 , Proposition 3 ] (see also [ CPGSV16 , Section 2.3 ] ).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.91Primitivity passes to a fixed corner

Let \(P\in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a nonzero idempotent and let \(E\) be a linear map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) that preserves the corner \(PM_{D}(\mathbb {C})P\), is primitive, and fixes the corner projection, \(E(P)=P\). Then the restriction of \(E\) to the corner \(PM_{D}(\mathbb {C})P\) is again primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.268Product span makes the sum of local spaces direct

Suppose

\[ \operatorname{span}\{ (A^1_w,\ldots ,A^r_w):|w|=n\} = \prod _jM_{D_j}(\mathbb {C}). \]

Then the sum of the local spaces \(G_n(A^j)\) is direct: if

\[ \phi _j\in G_n(A^j), \qquad \sum _j\phi _j=0, \]

then \(\phi _j=0\) for every \(j\). In particular, for the normalized BNT hypotheses above, this directness holds for every

\[ n\ge (L_0+1)+(r-1)3(L_0+1). \]

  • MPSTensor.groundSpace_iSupIndep_of_ge_of_bnt_directSum_unital_c1
Lemma 13.245Blockwise word span from block-separating equations

Let \(J\) be a finite set of \(r\) blocks, and let \((A_j)_{j\in J}\) be a finite family of tensors with common block injectivity at length \(L\). Suppose there are length-\(S\) block-separating equations: for each block \(k\),

\[ \sum _{|\omega |=S} c_\omega ^{(k)} A_j^\omega = \begin{cases} I, & j=k, \\ 0, & j\ne k. \end{cases} \]

Then the simultaneous length-\((L+S)\) block-word evaluations span \(\prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C})\). If the block-separating equations are first obtained from pairwise block-separating equations at length \(S\), the same conclusion holds at length \(L+(r-1)S\).

Lemma 7.10Trace product of positive semidefinite matrices

If \(A,B \geq 0\), then \(\operatorname{tr}(AB) \geq 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.185Quadratic form to norm bound

Let \(H\) be a positive semidefinite self-adjoint operator on a finite-dimensional complex Hilbert space, and suppose that, for all \(v\),

\[ \gamma \, \langle v, H v\rangle \le \langle H v, H v\rangle \]

i.e. \(H^2 \ge \gamma H\) as a quadratic form, for some \(\gamma {\gt} 0\). Then for every \(v\) in the orthogonal complement of \(\ker H\),

\[ \gamma \, \| v\| \le \| H v\| . \]

In particular, every positive eigenvalue of \(H\) is at least \(\gamma \). The positive-semidefiniteness hypothesis is essential: without it, \(H = -\mathrm{Id}\) with \(\gamma = 2\) satisfies the quadratic-form inequality vacuously but violates the norm bound.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.14Relative entropy with identical arguments

For every matrix \(\rho \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ D(\rho \Vert \rho )=0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.13Trace-log splitting for relative entropy

For matrices \(\rho ,\sigma \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ D(\rho \Vert \sigma ) = \operatorname {Re}\operatorname {tr}(\rho \log \rho ) - \operatorname {Re}\operatorname {tr}(\rho \log \sigma ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.15Relative entropy with zero first argument

For every matrix \(\sigma \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ D(0\Vert \sigma )=0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.49Rank-\(k\) Hermitian projection factorization

Let \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) be Hermitian, idempotent, and of rank \(k\). Then there exists \(V\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) such that

\[ P=VV^\dagger . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.45Rank-\(k\) projection fixing a rectangular right factor

Let \(X\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) and suppose \(k\le D\). Then there exists a Hermitian projection \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) of rank \(k\) such that

\[ PX=X. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.40Rank-one ampliation as Choi compression

Applying the \(k\)-fold ampliation of \(T\) to the rank-one matrix \(|\psi \rangle \langle \psi |\), where \(\psi _{(a,p)}=D^{-1/2}X_{a,p}\), is exactly the right-factor compression of the Choi matrix of \(T\) by \(X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.37Rank-one extraction from a non-invertible eigenvector

Let \(A\) be a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D \ge 1\). Suppose that a Kraus operator \(A^{i_0}\) is non-invertible and that there exists a vector \(\varphi \in \mathbb {C}^D\) and a nonzero scalar \(\mu \) such that \(A^{i_0}\varphi =\mu \varphi \). Then, for every \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\),

\[ |\varphi \rangle \! \langle \psi | \in S_{D^2 - D + 1}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.20Coefficient-matrix form of the reduced density

Writing \(C_i\) for the coefficient matrix of \(\phi _i\), the reduced density operator factors as \(\rho _i = C_i C_i^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.21Reduced density is positive semidefinite

The reduced density operator \(\rho _i\) is positive semidefinite.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.29Reindexing invariance of the quantum relative entropy

For Hermitian matrices \(\rho , \sigma \) on a finite index set and any bijection \(e\) from that set onto another finite set,

\[ D(\rho _{e^{-1},e^{-1}} \, \Vert \, \sigma _{e^{-1},e^{-1}}) = D(\rho \, \Vert \, \sigma ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.15Replacing an extracted window

If \(L \le N\), then prescribing on a cyclic interval the values already carried by \(\sigma \) leaves \(\sigma \) unchanged:

\[ \sigma ^{[i,i+L-1]\leftarrow \sigma |_{[i,i+L-1]}}=\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.34Schmidt rank under the adjoint right tensor factor

Let \(X\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) and let \(\eta \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^k\). Then \(R_X^\dagger \eta \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^D\) has Schmidt rank at most \(k\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • ChoiJamiolkowski.schmidtCoeffMatrix_rightTensorMatrix_conjTranspose_mulVec
  • ChoiJamiolkowski.rightTensorMatrix_conjTranspose_mulVec_hasSchmidtRankLE
Lemma 5.35Right-tensor representatives of bounded Schmidt rank

If \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^D\) has Schmidt rank at most \(k\), then there exist \(X\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\eta \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^k\) such that

\[ \psi =R_X^\dagger \eta . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.46Right-tensor factorization for \(VV^\dagger \)

For every \(V\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\), the right-tensor factor satisfies

\[ R_{VV^\dagger }=R_V^\dagger R_V. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.43Right-tensor multiplication

Let \(X\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) and \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\). Then the right-tensor factors satisfy

\[ R_XR_P=R_{PX}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.26Root-of-unity character-sum orthogonality

Let \(\zeta \) be a primitive \(d\)-th root of unity and let \(i, j\) range over the residues modulo \(d\). Then

\[ \sum _{b=0}^{d-1} \zeta ^{b\, i}\, \overline{\zeta ^{b\, j}} = \begin{cases} d & i = j, \\ 0 & i \ne j. \end{cases} \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 3.2Same matrix-product vectors give trace agreement

If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same matrix-product vectors, then \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) = \operatorname{tr}(B^w)\) for every word \(w\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.15Positive-length equality with equal bond dimensions

If two tensors have the same positive-length MPV family and the same bond dimension, then they have the same MPV family at every length.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.35Evaluation formula

\(E_\sigma (X)=\dfrac {\operatorname{tr}(\sigma X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )} \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.39Fixes scalar operators

For \(c\in \mathbb {C}\), \(E_\sigma (c\, \mathbb {1})=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.42Image lies in adjoint fixed points

For all \(X\), \(E_\sigma (X)\) is fixed by \(T^*\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.38Range is scalar

For every \(X\), there exists \(c\in \mathbb {C}\) with \(E_\sigma (X)=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.15Monotonicity of bounded Schmidt rank

If \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le k\) and \(k\le l\), then \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le l\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.33Square parametrization of bounded Schmidt rank

Assume \(D{\gt}0\). A vector \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^D\) has Schmidt rank at most \(k\) if and only if there is a matrix \(X\in M_D(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) of rank at most \(k\) such that

\[ \psi _{(i,j)}=D^{-1/2}X_{i,j}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.28Raw two-layer sector coefficient identity

For every sector decomposition \(P\), length \(N\), and basis index \(j\),

\[ c_N^{(j)} = \sum _{q=1}^{r_j} \mu _{j,q}^N. \]

This is [ CPGSV16 , Section 2.3 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.3Full-basis coefficient identity from matched sectors

Let \(P\) and \(Q\) be BNT canonical forms with the same MPV family, matched by a bijection \(\beta :J(Q)\simeq J(P)\) with bond-dimension equalities and a gauge-phase equivalence between \(Q_k\) and \(P_{\beta (k)}\) for every \(k\). Then each sector carries a unit-modulus phase \(\zeta _k\), and eventually

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ft_coeff_identity} c_N^{(\beta (k))}(P)=\zeta _k^N\, c_N^{(k)}(Q). \end{equation}
3

This is the equal-MPV coefficient comparison of [ CPGSV16 , Appendix MPV proof, lines 1187–1188 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.4Full-basis coefficient identity from matched phases

Let \(P\) be a BNT canonical form, let \(Q\) be a sector decomposition, and assume \(P_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(Q_{\mathrm{tot}}\) have the same MPV family. Suppose that there is a bijection

\[ \beta : J(Q) \simeq J(P) \]

and scalars \(\zeta _k \in \mathbb {C}\) such that, for every \(k \in J(Q)\), every length \(N\), and every spin configuration \(\sigma \) of length \(N\),

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ft_phase_relation_hyp} V^{(N)}(Q_k)_\sigma = \zeta _k^N\, V^{(N)}(P_{\beta (k)})_\sigma . \end{equation}
4

Then for every \(k \in J(Q)\) there is \(N_0\) such that, for all \(N{\gt}N_0\),

\[ c_N^{(\beta (k))}(P) = \zeta _k^N\, c_N^{(k)}(Q). \]

This is the coefficient-comparison step of [ CPGSV16 , Appendix MPV proof, lines 1187–1188 ] in full-basis form.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.11Proportional matched-phase coefficient identity, scalar-threaded

Let \(P\) be a BNT canonical form and \(Q\) a sector decomposition whose total tensors generate eventually nonzero proportional MPV families. Suppose a bijection \(\beta :J(Q)\simeq J(P)\) and scalars \(\zeta _k\) satisfy the matched MPV phase identities (??) for every sector \(k\). Then there is a single eventually-nonzero scalar sequence \(c_N\) and a threshold \(N_0\) such that, for all \(N{\gt}N_0\) and every \(k\in J(Q)\),

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ft_coeff_identity_threaded} c_N^{(\beta (k))}(P)=c_N\, \zeta _k^N\, c_N^{(k)}(Q). \end{equation}
7

The scalar \(c_N\) is the same for every sector \(k\): it is the single global proportionality scalar of [ CPGSV16 , Theorem thm1, lines 1167–1170 ] at length \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.44Sector coefficient is not eventually zero

For a BNT canonical form \(P\) and any sector \(j\), the sector coefficient \(c_{P,j}^{(N)} = \sum _q \mu _{j,q}^N\) is not eventually zero in \(N\). Only nonvanishing of the copy weights is used, not any modulus condition.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.30Combined-family eventual linear independence

Let \(P\) and \(Q\) each be a BNT canonical form, and suppose every mixed overlap \(O_{P_j Q_k}(N)\) tends to \(0\). Then the union family

\[ \bigl\{ |V^{(N)}(P_j)\rangle \bigr\} _{j=1}^{g_P} \cup \bigl\{ |V^{(N)}(Q_k)\rangle \bigr\} _{k=1}^{g_Q} \]

is linearly independent for all sufficiently large \(N\). This is [ CPGSV16 , Appendix A ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.6Copy-weight matching from matched-sector coefficient identities

Let the sectors of \(P\) and \(Q\) be matched by \(\beta :J(Q)\simeq J(P)\), with nonzero sector phases \(\zeta _k\). If the matched-sector coefficient identities

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ft_sector_coeff_identity_hyp} c_N^{(\beta (k))}(P)=\zeta _k^N\, c_N^{(k)}(Q) \end{equation}
5

hold eventually, then the phases \(\zeta _k\) determine a copy-weight matching \(\nu _{k,q}=\zeta _k^{-1}\mu _{\beta (k),\tau _k(q)}\). This is, sector by sector, [ CPGSV16 , Appendix MPV proof, line 1188 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.29Cross-overlap decay between distinct basis blocks

If \(P\) is a BNT canonical form and \(j \neq k\), then \(O_{P_j P_k}(N) \to 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.13Equal-MPV matched copy-weight witnesses

Let \(P\) and \(Q\) be BNT canonical forms whose total tensors have the same MPV family. Then there are a bijection \(\beta :J(Q)\simeq J(P)\), bond-dimension equalities, unit-modulus phases \(\zeta _k\), and block gauges \(X_k\) such that

\[ Q_k^i=\zeta _k X_kP_{\beta (k)}^iX_k^{-1}. \]

For the same phases there is a copy-weight matching: after a permutation of the copies inside each matched sector,

\[ \nu _{k,q}=\zeta _k^{-1}\mu _{\beta (k),\tau _k(q)}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.43Matched-sector weight-permutation extractor

Under the hypotheses of Lemma 10.42, the per-sector multiplicities agree and there is a permutation \(\tau \) of the per-copy index set identifying the individual weights up to the gauge phase: for every \(q\),

\[ \nu _{\tilde k_0, \tau (q)} = \zeta ^{-1} \cdot \mu _{j_0, q}. \]

This is the per-copy realisation of [ CPGSV16 , Appendix A ] : the multiset equality from Lemma 10.42 is upgraded to a concrete indexing between the matched \(P\)- and \(Q\)-copies.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.42Matched-sector weight multiset equality

Let \(P, Q\) be sector decompositions with weights \(\mu _{j,q}\) and \(\nu _{k,q'}\), and fix indices \(j_0\), \(\tilde k_0\), and a nonzero scalar \(\zeta \in \mathbb {C}\setminus \{ 0\} \). Suppose that for every \(N {\gt} N_0\),

\begin{equation} \label{eq:bnt_matched_sector_coeff_identity} c_N^{(j_0)}(P) = \zeta ^N \cdot c_N^{(\tilde k_0)}(Q). \end{equation}
2

Then the per-sector multiplicities agree, \(r_{j_0}(P) = r_{\tilde k_0}(Q)\), and the rescaled per-copy weight multisets coincide. This is [ CPGSV16 , Appendix A ] read on a single matched sector.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.1Unit phase from matched normalized BNT blocks

Let \(P\) and \(Q\) be BNT canonical forms. If, for every length \(N\) and word \(\sigma \), a sector \(P_j\) and a sector \(Q_k\) have MPV families related by

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ft_matched_mpv_phase_norm} V^{(N)}(Q_k)_\sigma =\zeta ^N V^{(N)}(P_j)_\sigma \end{equation}
1

then \(|\zeta |=1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.31Unit-modulus weight witness from the canonical-form normalization

Under the BNT canonical form, the [ CPGSV16 , Section 2.3 ] unit-modulus existential provides indices \((j_*, q_*)\) with \(|\mu _{j_*, q_*}| = 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.19Similarity is an involution

For any invertible \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and any linear map \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the similarity transforms by \(C\) and \(C^{-1}\) compose to the identity:

\[ X \mapsto C(C^{-1}\, E(C\, X\, C^\dagger )\, (C^\dagger )^{-1})\, C^\dagger = E(X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.18Similarity transform preserves irreducibility

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), let \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be invertible (\(\det C \neq 0\)), and let \(c {\gt} 0\). Define the similarity-transformed map

\[ E'(X) = c \cdot C^{-1}\, E\! (C\, X\, C^\dagger )\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}. \]

Then \(E'\) is also irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.20Irreducibility is invariant under similarity

For any invertible \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and any linear map \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the similarity transform \(X \mapsto C^{-1}\, E(C\, X\, C^\dagger )\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}\) is irreducible if and only if \(E\) is.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.44Square sandwich implies fixed rectangular compression

Let \(T:M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) have Choi matrix \(\tau \). Suppose \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) and \(X\in M_{D,k}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) satisfy \(PX=X\). If

\[ R_P\tau R_P^\dagger \geq 0, \]

then the rectangular right compression of \(\tau \) by \(X\) is positive semidefinite.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.45Intertwining of Stinespring isometries

Let \(K : \{ 0,\ldots ,r-1\} \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(L : \{ 0,\ldots ,s-1\} \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be two Kraus families. Write \(K\mathbin {+\! +}L\) for their concatenation as a family indexed by \(\{ 0,\ldots ,r+s-1\} \). Then

\[ V_K = (\mathbb {1}_D \otimes C_{r,s}) V_{K + \! + L}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.34Entry formula for the Stinespring isometry

For indices \(i,k \in \{ 0,\ldots ,D-1\} \) and \(j \in \{ 0,\ldots ,r-1\} \),

\[ V_{(i,j),k} = (K_j)_{ik}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.74Primitive tensors with positive-definite fixed point are strongly irreducible

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \), then \(A\) is strongly irreducible in the sense of  [ SPGWC10 , Proposition 3(c) ] : \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible, the fixed point \(\rho \) is positive definite, and the peripheral spectrum of \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is \(\{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.300Normalization for words

If

\[ \sum _a A_aA_a^\dagger =I , \]

then, for every length \(M\),

\[ \sum _\rho A_\rho A_\rho ^\dagger =I , \]

where \(\rho \) ranges over all words of length \(M\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.18Dyson-series summability from factorial majorant

Assume a factorial majorant of Dyson–Phillips iterates:

\[ \| D_n(t)\| \le M\, \frac{(t\, \| L'-L\| \, M)^n}{n!}. \]

Then the Dyson series is summable in operator norm.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.52Support projection annihilates the kernel

Let \(\rho \succeq 0\) on \(\mathbb {C}^D\) with support projection \(P = \mathrm{supp}(\rho )\). For every \(v \in \mathbb {C}^D\), if \(\rho \, v = 0\), then \(P\, v = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.236Finite-chain image inclusion from a three-block relation

Under the hypotheses of Lemma 13.235, the length-\(L\) spaces \(\mathcal G_L^A\) and \(\mathcal G_L^B\) satisfy

\[ \mathcal G_L^A \subseteq \mathcal G_L^B. \]

If the corresponding nonzero middle matrix exists on both sides, then \(\mathcal G_L^A=\mathcal G_L^B\).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.groundSpaceMap_eq_leftTraceWordMap
  • MPSTensor.groundSpace_eq_leftTraceWordMap_range
  • MPSTensor.leftTraceWordMap_range_le_of_three_block_trace_relation_left
  • MPSTensor.leftTraceWordMap_range_eq_of_three_block_trace_relation
  • MPSTensor.groundSpace_le_of_three_block_trace_relation_left
  • MPSTensor.groundSpace_eq_of_three_block_trace_relation
Lemma 13.235Three-block trace reduction from a nonzero middle matrix

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be tensors with virtual bond dimensions \(D_A\) and \(D_B\). Assume that \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective. Let \(\Delta _A\in M_{D_A}(\mathbb {C})\) with \(\Delta _A\neq 0\), and let \(\Delta _B\in M_{D_B}(\mathbb {C})\). Suppose that, for all words \(u,v,t\) of length \(L\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\! (\Delta _A A_v A_t A_u) + \operatorname{tr}\! (\Delta _B B_v B_t B_u)=0. \]

Then for every \(Z\in M_{D_A}(\mathbb {C})\) there is a matrix \(W\in M_{D_B}(\mathbb {C})\) such that, for all words \(t\) of length \(L\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}(ZA_t)+\operatorname{tr}(WB_t)=0. \]

This is the trace-dual algebraic step in the two-block case of the direct-sum lemma of [ PGVWC07 ] .

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.exists_right_trace_test_of_three_block_trace_relation_left
  • MPSTensor.exists_left_trace_test_of_three_block_trace_relation_right
Lemma 6.16Square-root trace-preserving gauge is a gauge equivalence

If \(\sigma \) is positive definite and \(B^i=\sigma ^{1/2}A^i\sigma ^{-1/2}\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.5Trace expansion over matrix units

For any linear endomorphism \(T\) on \(M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C})\), the operator trace can be expanded as

\[ \operatorname{Tr}(T) = \sum _{p=0}^{D_1-1} \sum _{q=0}^{D_2-1} (T(E_{pq}))_{pq}, \]

where \(E_{pq} \in M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C})\) is the matrix with \(1\) in position \((p,q)\) and \(0\) elsewhere.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.37Partial trace preserves Hermiticity

If \(\rho _{ABC}\) is Hermitian, then \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{ABC})\), \(\operatorname{tr}_C(\rho _{ABC})\), and \(\operatorname{tr}_{AC}(\rho _{ABC})\) are all Hermitian. The same holds for bipartite partial traces \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{AB})\) and \(\operatorname{tr}_B(\rho _{AB})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.83Trace pairing for Kraus map and adjoint map

For all \(X, Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\! (X\, E(Y)) = \operatorname{tr}\! (E^*(X)\, Y). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 3.10An injective tensor forces a nonzero partner

Let \(D \ge 1\). If \(A\) is injective and \(A\), \(B\) generate the same matrix-product vectors, then the \(B^i\) are not all zero.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.62Iterated transfer does not vanish

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then \(\mathcal{E}_A^n \neq 0\) for all \(n \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.176Hilbert-space local terms are symmetric projections

The Hilbert-space local projector \(P_L^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\) is a symmetric projection, and every Hilbert-space local term \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) on the \(N\)-site chain is a symmetric projection.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentInteractionES_isSymmetricProjection
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_isSymmetricProjection
Lemma 13.184Non-overlap positivity for Hilbert-space local terms

Let \(L\le N\) and let \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}},h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\) be Hilbert-space local terms whose cyclic \(L\)-windows are site-disjoint: no site of the periodic chain has cyclic offset \({\lt}L\) from both \(i\) and \(j\). Then the terms commute pointwise: for every vector \(v\),

\[ h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v) = h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v), \]

and their ordered cross term is non-negative:

\[ 0\le \operatorname {Re}\langle h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v, h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\rangle . \]
LaTeX

Lemma 13.175Positivity of the Hilbert-space Hamiltonian

Each Hilbert-space local term \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}\) is positive, hence the full Hilbert-space parent Hamiltonian \(H_{N,\mathrm{ES}}\) is positive.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.localTermES_isPositive
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianES_isPositive
Lemma 9.57Single-copy sector decomposition preserves the represented family

The single-copy sector decomposition of \((\mu _k,B_k)_k\) represents the same MPS family as \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.28Twirl as partial trace tensored with the maximally mixed ancilla

Fix a dimension \(d_C \ge 1\) and a primitive \(d_C\)-th root of unity \(\zeta \). For every matrix \(M\) on \(\mathcal H_S \otimes \mathbb {C}^{d_C}\), the uniform average of the conjugations by the \(d_C^2\) unitaries \(\mathbf1_S \otimes W(a,b)\) on the second factor is the partial trace over that factor tensored with the maximally mixed state \(\mathbf1_C / d_C\):

\[ \frac{1}{d_C^2} \sum _{a,b} (\mathbf1_S \otimes W(a,b))\, M\, (\mathbf1_S \otimes W(a,b))^{\dagger } = (\operatorname{tr}_C M) \otimes (\mathbf1_C / d_C). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.5Functoriality of twisting

The twisted tensor satisfies the composition law

\[ \widetilde{A}_{gh} = \widetilde{(\widetilde{A}_h)}_g, \]

i.e. twisting by \(gh\) is the same as first twisting by \(h\) and then by \(g\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.6Identity twist

Twisting by the identity group element is trivial: \(\widetilde{A}_1 = A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.50Twisted transfer as a mixed transfer operator

The twisted transfer map of \(A\) is the mixed transfer operator of the pair \((A,B_u)\):

\[ \mathcal{E}_u = F_{A,B_u}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.64One matrix \(Y_\mu \) from compared boundary matrices

Fix a physical letter \(\eta \) used outside the complementary sites. Suppose the two one-sided boundary matrix families \(Y^+\) and \(Y^-\) have been extracted from the cyclic windows. If, for every word on the complementary sites \(\mu \), these boundary matrices agree on the boundary conditions built from \(\eta \),

\[ Y^+_{\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )} = Y^-_{\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )}, \]

then there is a single family \(Y_\mu \) satisfying

\[ A^\mu A^b X = Y_\mu A^b, \qquad X A^a A^\mu = A^a Y_\mu \]

for all letters \(a,b\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.10Two-sided span from one nonzero matrix

If \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is nonzero, then

\begin{equation} \label{eq:wld_two_sided_nonzero_span} \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ R C S : R, S \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\} =M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \end{equation}
1

This is the matrix-factorization input used in  [ PGVWC07 , Lemma 3 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.267Normalized block-separating equations give all large product spans

Let \(A^1,\ldots ,A^r\) be blocks satisfying the normalization

\[ \sum _a A^j_aA^{j\, \dagger }_a=I \]

and assume that every block is injective at length \(L\):

\[ \operatorname{span}\{ A^j_u:|u|=L\} =M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C}). \]

If there are block-separating equations of length \(S\), then, for every \(n\ge L+(r-1)S\),

\[ \operatorname{span}\{ (A^1_w,\ldots ,A^r_w):|w|=n\} = \prod _j M_{D_j}(\mathbb {C}). \]

In particular, for normalized BNT representatives satisfying the displayed unital normalization, if \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(\Gamma _{L_0}^{A^j}\) is injective for every block \(j\), the same conclusion holds with

\[ S=3(L_0+1), \qquad n\ge (L_0+1)+(r-1)3(L_0+1). \]
LaTeX

Lemma 12.46Unitary Kraus mixing

If \(u\) is unitary, then replacing Kraus operators \(\{ A_i\} \) by their unitary mixtures \(\{ \sum _j u_{ij} A_j\} \) preserves the channel:

\[ \sum _i \left(\sum _j u_{ij} A_j\right)\, Y \left(\sum _j u_{ij} A_j\right)^\dagger = \sum _i A_i\, Y\, A_i^\dagger . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.25Vector span from matrix span

If \(T_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\varphi \neq 0\), then \(K_N(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.80Amplifying fixed-length word commutation

If a boundary matrix \(X\) commutes with every word product of length \(m\), then it commutes with every word product whose length is a multiple \(q\, m\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.53Chunking: word span embeds into blocked word span

\(S_{nL}(A) \subseteq S_n(A^{[L]})\) for all \(n, L\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.44Word span products

\(S_m(A) \cdot S_n(A) \subseteq S_{m+n}(A)\): the product of word spans at lengths \(m\) and \(n\) is contained in the word span at length \(m + n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.7Word span characterises block injectivity

\(S_L(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) if and only if \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.8Zero-length word span

If \(D \ge 2\), then \(S_0(A) \ne M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.56Common complement word for the two boundary supports

On a chain of length \(N=M+1\), with \(L_0\le M\), fix a physical letter \(\eta \) used outside the complementary sites and a word \(\mu \) of length \(M+1-(L_0+1)\). Write \(\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )\) for the boundary condition at the support crossing the last site and \(\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )\) for the boundary condition at the second boundary-crossing support. Their exposed complements are both exactly \(\mu \).

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.mirrorMiddleBackground
  • MPSTensor.wrappedMiddleBackground_complement
  • MPSTensor.mirrorMiddleBackground_complement
Lemma 13.97Products with one-site tensors determine the boundary matrix

Let \(A\) be \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\). Fix two matrix families \(Y^+\) and \(Y^-\) and the two reindexed boundary conditions \(\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )\) and \(\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )\). If, for every physical letter \(j\),

\[ Y^+_{\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )} A^j = Y^-_{\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )} A^j, \]

then

\[ Y^+_{\tau ^+_\eta (\mu )} = Y^-_{\tau ^-_\eta (\mu )}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 13.51Boundary-crossing compatibility at the last site

Assume \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(M \ge L_0\). If the reduced cyclic interval crossing the last site has boundary matrices \(Y_\tau \), then for every physical letter \(j\) one has

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_wrapped_window_compat} C^+_\tau A^j X = Y_\tau A^j, \end{equation}
7

where \(C^+_\tau \) is the complementary word seen by that cyclic interval.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.183Intersection property for two adjacent local kernels

If \(A\) is injective and \(L\ge 2\), then on an \((L+1)\)-site chain the kernels of the two adjacent Hilbert-space \(L\)-site local terms have common kernel equal to the Hilbert-space \((L+1)\)-site MPS ground space:

\[ h_{0,\mathrm{ES}}v=0\ \text{ and }\ h_{1,\mathrm{ES}}v=0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad v\in G_{L+1}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A). \]
LaTeX
Lean

  • MPSTensor.adjacent_localTermES_eq_zero_of_mem_groundSpaceES_succ
  • MPSTensor.adjacent_localTermES_eq_zero_iff_mem_groundSpaceES_succ
Theorem 9.49Adjoint-fixed projections give sector isometries

Let \(A\) be left-canonical and let \((P_k)\) be orthogonal projections summing to \(\mathbb {1}\) and fixed by the adjoint transfer map. Then the same sector decomposition and support isometries of Theorem 9.48 exist. In particular, the adjoint fixed-point condition \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (P_k)=P_k\) gives

\[ P_k A^i=A^i P_k, \]

which gives the corner-letter identity

\[ \varphi _k(C_k^i)=P_k A^i P_k. \]
Proof ▶

The fixed-point equation \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (P_k)=P_k\), together with left-canonicality \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i=\mathbb {1}\), places \(P_k\) in the multiplicative domain of \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \), forcing

\[ P_k A^i=A^i P_k \]

for every letter \(A^i\). The result then follows from Theorem 9.48.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.23Adjoint transfer map is nonzero

If some Kraus operator \(A^i \neq 0\), then the adjoint transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (X) = \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger X A^i\) is nonzero.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.88Adjoint fixed points equal the Kraus commutant

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 4.86, the adjoint fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra equals the Kraus commutant:

\[ \mathrm{Fix}(E^*) = \{ K_i, K_i^\dagger \} '. \]

This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 6.13 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.59Fixed-point algebra decomposition with multiplicities

There exist integers \(n\), block sizes \(d_1,\ldots ,d_n \ge 1\), multiplicities \(m_1,\ldots ,m_n \ge 1\), and a \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra isomorphism from the adjoint-fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra to

\[ \prod _{k=1}^{n} M_{d_k}(\mathbb {C}) \]

such that

\[ \sum _{k=1}^{n} d_k m_k \le D. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.86Fixed points form a \(*\)-subalgebra in the Heisenberg picture

If the Kraus map is trace-preserving and has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\) with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then \(\mathrm{Fix}(E^*)\) is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • Kraus.adjointFixedPointsStarSubalgebra
  • Kraus.fixedPoints_starSubalgebra
Theorem 17.102Adjoint kernel lies in the commutant

Let \(L\) be a GKSL generator in Lindblad form with Hamiltonian \(H\) and Lindblad operators \(\{ L_j\} \). Define the adjoint generator \(L^*\) by \(\operatorname {tr}(\rho \, L^*(A)) = \operatorname {tr}(L(\rho )\, A)\). If \(L\) has a faithful stationary state \(\rho _0 {\gt} 0\) with \(L(\rho _0) = 0\), then for any \(A\):

\[ L^*(A) = 0 \implies [A,H] = [A,L_j] = [A,L_j^\dagger ] = 0\ \forall j. \]

That is, \(\ker (L^*) \subseteq \{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} '\). This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 7.2 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.103Adjoint kernel equals the commutant

Under the same hypotheses as Theorem 17.102,

\[ \ker (L^*) = \{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} ', \]

i.e. the adjoint kernel equals the commutant of \(\{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} \). This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 7.2 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.41Adjoint map absorbs scalar projection

If \(T\) is trace-preserving, then \(T^*\circ E_\sigma = E_\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.25The blocked AKLT tensor is injective

The length-\(2\) blocked AKLT tensor is injective: its nine matrices span \(M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.26\(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\) on-site symmetry of the blocked AKLT tensor

The length-\(2\) blocked AKLT tensor is on-site symmetric under \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\), with each group element acting by the Kronecker square \(P_g \otimes P_g\) of the corresponding single-site spin-\(1\) \(\pi \)-rotation \(P_g\) (Definition 15.24).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.35AKLT correlation length

The AKLT correlation length, computed from the subleading eigenvalue \(\lambda _2 = -\tfrac 13\), equals

\[ \xi = -\frac{1}{\log |\lambda _2|} = \frac{1}{\log 3}. \]

It is finite and positive.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.aklt_correlationLength_eq
  • MPSTensor.aklt_correlationLength_pos
Theorem 15.17Anticommuting virtual gauges of the AKLT symmetry

The two virtual matrices that implement the symmetry of Theorem 15.16, namely \(i\sigma _y\) and \(\sigma _z\), anticommute.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.21The AKLT \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\) phase is non-trivial

The AKLT factor system (Theorem 15.20), built from the anticommuting gauges \(i\sigma _y\) and \(\sigma _z\) (Theorem 15.17), is not a coboundary: its commutator phase on the two generators is \(-1\). Its class in \(H^2(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2, \mathrm{U}(1)) \cong \mathbb {Z}_2\) is therefore non-trivial, so the AKLT chain realizes a non-trivial SPT phase rather than a trivial product state.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.15AKLT tensor is normal

The transfer map of the AKLT tensor has a unique eigenvalue of maximal modulus (a simple spectral-radius eigenvalue); in particular the AKLT tensor is normal. Concretely, the length-\(2\) blocked tensor is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.14AKLT tensor is not injective

The AKLT tensor is not injective at a single site: \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^0, A^1, A^2\} \) is the three-dimensional space of traceless matrices \(\mathfrak {sl}(2,\mathbb {C})\), which is a proper subspace of \(M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.20The AKLT factor system is a \(2\)-cocycle

The AKLT factor system is a genuine \(2\)-cocycle, being the factor system of an explicit projective representation, so it represents an element of \(H^2(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2, \mathrm{U}(1))\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.33Pauli eigenvectors of the AKLT transfer map

Each of the three Pauli matrices \(\sigma _x\), \(\sigma _y\), \(\sigma _z\) is an eigenvector of the AKLT transfer map with eigenvalue \(-\tfrac 13\):

\[ \mathcal{E}_A(\sigma _k) = -\tfrac 13\, \sigma _k, \qquad k \in \{ x, y, z\} . \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.aklt_transferMap_sigmaZ
  • MPSTensor.aklt_transferMap_sigmaX
  • MPSTensor.aklt_transferMap_sigmaY
Theorem 15.31\(\mathrm{SO}(3)\) on-site symmetry of the AKLT tensor

The Cartesian AKLT tensor is on-site symmetric under the spin-\(1\) (defining) representation of \(\mathrm{SO}(3)\) on the physical index.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.28The AKLT state has string order under its \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\) symmetry

For every element \(g\) of \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\), the length-\(2\) blocked AKLT tensor has string order in the sense of Definition 12.40 for the twist by \(g\), with the maximally mixed boundary state \(\Lambda = \tfrac {1}{2}\mathbb {1}\) as its stationary boundary state.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.34Subleading eigenvalue of the AKLT transfer map

The value \(1\) is the leading eigenvalue of the AKLT transfer map, with the identity \(\mathbb {1}\) as eigenvector, and \(-\tfrac 13\) is a subleading eigenvalue, with \(\sigma _z\) as eigenvector. The subleading modulus is therefore \(\tfrac 13\).

LaTeX
Lean
