  • MPSTensor.localTerm_two_three_zero_one_commute_of_appendixD2
Theorem 18.19Map form of Jensen for the concave logarithm

For a positive unital map \(T\) and a positive-definite matrix \(A\), the inequality \(T(\log A) \le \log (TA)\) holds.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.92Lorentz normal form for qubit channels

For every qubit channel \(T : M_{2}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\), there exist \(\operatorname{SL}(2, \mathbb {C})\)-filterings \(\Phi _{1}, \Phi _{2}\) such that the filtered channel \(T' = \Phi _{2} \circ T \circ \Phi _{1}\) is in one of the three Lorentz normal forms: diagonal, non-diagonal, or singular. Not yet proved—proof still needs the generic normal form and the Lorentz group classification of \(\operatorname{SL}(2, \mathbb {C})\) orbits.

LaTeX
Theorem 9.9Invariant projection gives a two-block decomposition

Suppose \(P\) is an orthogonal projection satisfying, for every physical index \(i\),

\[ (\mathbb {1}- P) A^i P = 0. \]

Then there exist \(n,m\) with \(n + m = D\) and MPS tensors \(A_1, A_2\) of bond dimensions \(n\) and \(m\) such that \(A\) and the two-block tensor \(A_1 \oplus A_2\) generate the same MPV family in the sense of Definition 2.12.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.10Nontrivial invariant projection gives strict two-block reduction

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 9.9, assume in addition that \(P \neq 0\) and \(P \neq \mathbb {1}\). Then there exist \(n,m\) with \(n + m = D\), \(n {\lt} D\), and \(m {\lt} D\), together with block tensors \(A_1, A_2\), such that \(A\) and \(A_1 \oplus A_2\) generate the same MPV family in the sense of Definition 2.12.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.42Two-fold dimer degeneracy of the Majumdar–Ghosh ground space

On an even periodic chain of length \(N = 2m\ge 4\), let \(H_{N,\mathrm{MG}}^{(3)}\) be the range-three parent Hamiltonian of the Majumdar–Ghosh tensor, the periodic sum of translates of the local projector with kernel \(\mathcal G_3(A_{\mathrm{MG}})\). Then \(H_{N,\mathrm{MG}}^{(3)}\) has a two-dimensional ground space, spanned by the two nearest-neighbour singlet coverings \((1,2)(3,4)\cdots (N-1,N)\) and \((2,3)(4,5)\cdots (N,1)\). This two-fold degeneracy reflects the spontaneous breaking of the one-site translation symmetry of the spin-\(\tfrac 12\) chain \(H=\sum \boldsymbol {S}_i\cdot \boldsymbol {S}_{i+1} +\tfrac 12\sum \boldsymbol {S}_i\cdot \boldsymbol {S}_{i+2}\) of [ CPGSV21 ] .

LaTeX
Theorem 15.37Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is in left-canonical form

The Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is isometric: \((A^0)^\dagger A^0 + (A^1)^\dagger A^1 = \mathbb 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.39Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is not injective

The Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is not injective: every element of \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^0, A^1\} \) has vanishing \((2,2)\) entry, so the span is a proper subspace of \(M_{3}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.40Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is not normal

The Majumdar–Ghosh tensor is not normal: no blocking length makes the products of that length span the full matrix algebra \(M_{3}(\mathbb {C})\). This non-normality is the algebraic signature expected from the two-periodic dimer-covering structure.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.38Transfer map of the Majumdar–Ghosh tensor

The transfer map of the Majumdar–Ghosh tensor sends the identity to the diagonal matrix \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb 1) = \operatorname{diag}(2, \tfrac 12, \tfrac 12)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.80Projected elements lie in the multiplicative domain

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map whose adjoint map has a positive definite fixed point, and suppose \(E^2 = E\). Then \(E(X)\) lies in the multiplicative domain of \(E\) for every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.212Martingale criterion for spectral gap

Consider a frustration-free Hamiltonian

\[ H = \sum _{i=1}^n h_i \]

with projector interaction terms (\(h_i^2 = h_i\)). Suppose that for every unordered pair \(\{ i,j\} \) of overlapping terms there exist constants \(c_{\{ i,j\} } \ge 0\) and \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) satisfying

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_martingale_condition} h_i h_j + h_j h_i \ge -c_{\{ i,j\} }(1-\gamma )(h_i + h_j), \end{equation}
21

and suppose further that the coefficients attached to the terms overlapping \(h_i\) satisfy

\[ \sum _{\substack {j\ne i\\ \{ i,j\} \text{ overlapping}}}c_{\{ i,j\} }\le 1 \]

for every \(i\). Then the smallest nonzero eigenvalue of \(H\) satisfies \(\lambda _{\min }^+(H) \ge \gamma \).

LaTeX
Theorem 5.17Maximal overlap with a fixed Schmidt rank

Let \(\phi \in \mathbb {C}^{D}\otimes \mathbb {C}^k\) be a normalized vector with reduced density matrix \(\rho =\operatorname{tr}_2 |\phi \rangle \langle \phi |\) on the first factor, and let \(1\le n{\lt}D\). The largest squared overlap \(|\langle \phi | \psi \rangle |^2\) attained by a normalized vector \(\psi \) of Schmidt rank at most \(n\) is the Ky-Fan \(n\)-norm of \(\rho \):

\[ \max _{\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le n,\ \| \psi \| =1} |\langle \phi | \psi \rangle |^2 = \| \rho \| _{(n)} . \]

Because \(\rho \) is positive semidefinite, this norm is the sum of its \(n\) largest eigenvalues, which coincide there with its singular values. This is [ Wol12 , Lemma 3.1 ] for \(1\le n{\lt}D\); the top index \(n=D\), where the value is \(\operatorname{tr}\rho =1\), is Theorem 5.18.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.18Maximal overlap at the top Schmidt rank

At the top index \(n=D\) the Schmidt-rank constraint \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le D\) is vacuous, so the largest squared overlap is the Ky-Fan \(D\)-norm of \(\rho \),

\[ \max _{\| \psi \| =1} |\langle \phi | \psi \rangle |^2 = \| \rho \| _{(D)} , \]

attained at \(\psi =\phi \). The Ky-Fan \(D\)-norm sums all eigenvalues, so for a normalized \(\phi \) it collapses to \(\| \rho \| _{(D)}=\operatorname{tr}\rho =1\). Together with Theorem 5.17 this covers the full range \(1\le n\le D\) of [ Wol12 , Lemma 3.1 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.93Multiplicative-domain characterization

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map. Then

\[ X \in \mathcal{A}_R(E) \iff E(X X^\dagger ) = E(X) E(X)^\dagger , \]

and

\[ X \in \mathcal{A}_L(E) \iff E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X). \]

Together these are the two one-sided characterizations from [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.7 ] .

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.mem_rightMultiplicativeDomain_iff
  • KadisonSchwarz.mem_leftMultiplicativeDomain_iff
Theorem 5.96Restriction to multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-homomorphism

If \(E\) is unital, then the restricted map \(E|_{\mathcal{A}(E)} : \mathcal{A}(E) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a \(*\)-algebra homomorphism. This is the homomorphism conclusion of [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.7 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.95Multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-subalgebra

For a unital Kraus map \(E\), the full multiplicative domain \(\mathcal{A}(E)\) is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). This is the algebraic content of [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.7 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.67Multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-subalgebra

For a unital Kraus map, the multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.167Ground-space membership

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_K v = v \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad P_{AX} v = v \text{ and } P_{XB} v = v. \]

This is the algebraic consequence corresponding to equation (D.2) from [ CPGSV16 ] : \(K_{AXB} = (K_{AX} \otimes H_B) \cap (H_A \otimes K_{XB})\). The source equation itself also carries the tensor-product local-subspace hypotheses.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.4Iterated mixed transfer

For all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(N \ge 0\),

\[ F_{AB}^N(X) = \sum _{\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N} A^\sigma X (B^\sigma )^\dagger , \]

where \(A^\sigma = A^{\sigma _1} \cdots A^{\sigma _N}\) denotes the word evaluation (Definition 2.2).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.3Self-transfer

Setting \(B=A\) recovers the ordinary transfer map: \(F_{AA} = \mathcal{E}_A\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.16Spectral radius \(\ge 1\) implies gauge-phase equivalence

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized MPS tensors. If \(\rho _{\operatorname{spec}}(F_{AB}) \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.28Gauge-equivalence from irreducible-TP spectral radius

Let \(A, B\) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized MPS tensors of bond dimension \(D\). If \(\rho _{\operatorname{spec}}(F_{AB}) \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 2.41MPV decomposition

The MPV of a block-diagonal tensor decomposes as

\[ |V^{(N)}(A)\rangle = \sum _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k^N |V^{(N)}(A_k)\rangle . \]

Equivalently, for each configuration \(\sigma \) one has

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = \sum _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k^N V^{(N)}(A_k)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.75Periodic MPS vector nonvanishing for block-injective tensors

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0 {\gt} 0\), \(D \ge 1\), and \(N \ge L_0 + 1\), then \(V^{(N)}(A) \ne 0\) in the \(N\)-site space.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.70Common-period corner preservation

If \(N \in \mathbb {N}\) is divisible by every per-cycle period \(m(c)\), then \(T^N\) preserves every corner \(P_{c,k} M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\, P_{c,k}\) of the decomposition.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.69Per-cycle corner preservation

For every cycle \(c \in \iota \) and index \(k \in \{ 0,\ldots ,m(c){-}1\} \), the iterate \(T^{m(c)}\) preserves the corner \(P_{c,k} M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\, P_{c,k}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.53Elementary area-law bound on mutual information

For a bipartite density matrix \(\rho _{AB}\) on \(\mathbb {C}^{d_A} \otimes \mathbb {C}^{d_B}\) with \(d_A, d_B \ge 1\) whose single-system reduced states are obtained by partial trace,

\[ I(A{:}B) \le \log d_A + \log d_B. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.52Mutual information is monotone under enlargement

For any tripartite density matrix \(\rho _{ABC}\) on \(A \otimes B \otimes C\),

\[ I(A{:}B)_{\operatorname{tr}_C \rho _{ABC}} \le I(A{:}BC)_{\rho _{ABC}}, \]

where the left-hand side is the bipartite mutual information of the reduced state \(\rho _{AB} = \operatorname{tr}_C(\rho _{ABC})\) and the right-hand side is evaluated by expanding \(I(A{:}BC) = S(\rho _A) + S(\rho _{BC}) - S(\rho _{ABC})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.44Mutual information is non-negative

For any bipartite density matrix \(\rho _{AB}\), \(I(A{:}B) \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.68Concrete uniqueness for canonical projectors

Let \(V : \mathbb {C}^D \to \mathbb {C}^D \otimes \mathbb {C}^n\) satisfy \(V^\dagger P_i V = E_i\) for the canonical projectors \(P_i = \mathbb {1}_D \otimes |i\rangle \! \langle i|\). Then there exists an isometry \(W\) on the dilated space such that \(V = W V_0\), where \(V_0\) is the canonical Naimark isometry of \(\{ E_i\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.62Naimark isometry condition

The Naimark isometry satisfies \(V^\dagger V = \mathbb {1}_D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.63Naimark projectors form a projective measurement

The Naimark projectors satisfy \(P_i^2 = P_i\), \(P_i^\dagger = P_i\), \(P_i P_j = 0\) for \(i \ne j\), and \(\sum _i P_i = \mathbb {1}_{\mathbb {C}^D \otimes \mathbb {C}^n}\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • POVM.naimarkProjection_mul_self
  • POVM.naimarkProjection_hermitian
  • POVM.naimarkProjection_orthogonal
  • POVM.naimarkProjection_sum_eq_one
Theorem 16.64Naimark dilation

Every POVM \(\{ E_i\} _{i=0}^{n-1}\) arises as a projective measurement on a dilation: for the isometry \(V\) and projectors \(P_i\) above,

\[ E_i = V^\dagger P_i V. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.27Self-overlap is derived in normal canonical form

If \((\mu _k, A_k)\) satisfies the normal canonical form conditions, then \(O_{A_k A_k}(N) \to 1\) for every block \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.19Newton–Girard trace recursion

If \(A, B \in M_{n}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfy \(\operatorname{tr}(A^k) = \operatorname{tr}(B^k)\) for all \(k \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) have the same characteristic polynomial. It is enough to assume this equality for \(1 \le k \le n\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • Matrix.charpoly_eq_of_forall_trace_pow_eq
  • Matrix.charpoly_eq_of_trace_pow_eq_of_le_card
Theorem 8.14Nilpotent power bound

If \(f\) is nilpotent on a space of dimension \(n\), then \(f^n = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.151All-chain nearest-neighbor commuting parent-Hamiltonian ground spaces imply a renormalization fixed point

Let \(B\) be normal and in left-canonical form. Suppose that the family \(A_j\) is a BNT for \(B\), and that the nearest-neighbor parent Hamiltonian of \(B\) has the all-chain ground-space property of Definition 13.138: for every \(N{\gt}2\) the length-two translated parent interactions commute,

\[ h_i(B,2)h_j(B,2)=h_j(B,2)h_i(B,2), \]

the vector \(V^{(N)}(B)\) has zero energy, and

\[ \ker H_2^{(N)}(B) = \operatorname {span}\{ V^{(N)}(A_j):j=1,\ldots ,g\} . \]

Then \(B\) is a renormalization fixed point. At present this reverse implication remains an unproved hypothesis, representing Beigi’s one-dimensional nearest-neighbor commuting-Hamiltonian ground-space theorem and the identification of the resulting locally orthogonal states with the BNT of \(B\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.nncph_implies_rfp
  • Axioms.beigi_nncph_to_rfp
Theorem 12.29Commutator-phase non-triviality test

If a commuting pair \(g,h\) has \(\beta _\omega (g,h)\neq 1\), then \(\omega \) has a non-trivial class.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.17Nonzero trace product

If \(A\) is normal, then there exists a word \(w\) of length \(\le D^2\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This is  [ SPGWC10 , Lemma 1 ] . For the trace argument below, the nontrivial case is \(D \ge 1\); when \(D = 0\) the normality hypothesis is vacuous.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.19Sharp nonzero trace product

If \(A\) is normal, then there exists a word \(w\) of length \(\le D^2 - \operatorname{kr}(A) + 1\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This is  [ SPGWC10 , Lemma 1 ] with the sharp length bound.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.20Sharp nonzero trace product — positive length

If \(A\) is normal and \(D \ge 2\), then there exists a word \(w\) of positive length \(1 \le |w| \le D^2 - \operatorname{kr}(A) + 1\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This strengthens Theorem 8.19 by requiring \(|w| \ge 1\), a feature needed for the blocking argument in the general Wielandt bound below.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.23Factorial bound on the Dyson remainder

For \(s \in [0,t]\), \(M = \sup _{u \in [0,t]}\| e^{uL}\| \), and \(M' = \sup _{u \in [0,t]}\| e^{uL'}\| \):

\[ \bigl\| T'_s - \textstyle \sum _{n {\lt} N}\widetilde{T}^{(n)}_s\bigr\| \le M'\, \frac{(s\, \| \Delta \| \, M)^N}{N!}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.19Factorial norm bound for Dyson iterates

For \(s \in [0,t]\) and \(M = \sup _{u \in [0,t]} \| e^{uL}\| \),

\[ \| \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_s\| \le M\, \frac{(s\, \| \Delta \| \, M)^n}{n!}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.75Primitive MPS with positive-definite fixed point is normal

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \), then \(A\) is normal (has eventually full Kraus rank). No aperiodicity assumption is needed.

This is the key connection between primitivity in Definition 8.59, formulated via a complementary transfer-map gap, and normality; it removes the need for the aperiodicity-based argument.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.46Open-chain iteration of the intersection property

Let \(A\) be \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(D \ge 1\) and \(L_0 {\gt} 0\). If an \(N\)-site state satisfies the ground-space constraint on every non-wrapping contiguous interval of length \(L_0+1\), for every fixed choice of the complementary sites, with \(N \ge L_0+1\), then it lies in \(G_N(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.123Normal tensor: \(\mathcal G_{N,L}(A)\subseteq \operatorname{span}\{ V^{(N)}(A)\} \)

If \(A\) is normal, \(D \ge 1\), and injective after blocking \(L_0{\gt}0\) sites, with \(N \ge 2\), \(L_0+1{\lt}N\), and \(L_0 {\lt} L \le N\), then

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}(A) \subseteq \operatorname{span}\bigl\{ V^{(N)}(A)\bigr\} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.51TP-primitive irreducible tensor is normal

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\), irreducible transfer map, and peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \). Then \(A\) is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.18Non-equivalent irreducible TP blocks are eventually non-proportional

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be irreducible trace-preserving blocks whose self-overlaps converge to \(1\). If they are not gauge-phase equivalent, then for every lower bound \(N_0\) there is \(N \ge N_0\) such that \(|V^{(N)}(A)\rangle \) is not proportional to \(|V^{(N)}(B)\rangle \). Consequently, in an already separated same-dimensional BNT block family, any two distinct blocks have such non-proportional MPV states at arbitrarily large lengths.

LaTeX

  • MPSTensor.exists_ge_not_forall_mpv_eq_mul_of_blocksNotGaugePhaseEquiv_of_irreducible_TP
Theorem 17.130Full algebra generation implies non-reducibility

If the algebra generated by \(\{ L_j\} \) and \(\kappa \) is the entire matrix algebra \(M_d(\mathbb {C})\), then the QDS is not reducible. This is [ Wol12 , Corollary 7.2(1) ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.131Hermitian span and trivial commutant imply non-reducibility

If the Lindblad span is Hermitian-closed and its commutant is trivial, then the QDS is not reducible. This is [ Wol12 , Corollary 7.2(2) ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.132Large Kossakowski rank implies non-reducibility

If \(\operatorname{rank}(C) {\gt} d^2 - d\), then the QDS is not reducible. This is [ Wol12 , Corollary 7.2(3) ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.129No block-upper-triangular Lindblad form implies non-reducibility

If a GKSL generator admits no block-upper-triangular Lindblad form, then the associated quantum dynamical semigroup is not reducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.30Gauge-phase equivalence from unit-modulus overlap

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be irreducible MPS tensors of the same bond dimension with \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\). If \(\| \langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \| \to 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.51\(1\) is a peripheral eigenvalue

If \(E\) has a nonzero fixed point \(\rho \neq 0\) with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then \(1\) is a peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.63One-positive maps are positive

A linear map is \(1\)-positive if and only if it is positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.94One-sided multiplicative domains are subalgebras

For a unital Kraus map \(E\), both \(\mathcal{A}_R(E)\) and \(\mathcal{A}_L(E)\) are unital subalgebras of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.rightMultiplicativeDomainSubalgebra
  • KadisonSchwarz.leftMultiplicativeDomainSubalgebra
Theorem 18.4Operator convexity of real powers between one and two

For elements \(a,b\) of a unital C\({}^\ast \)-algebra with \(0\le a,b\), an exponent \(p\in [1,2]\), and \(t\in [0,1]\), the map \(a\mapsto a^p\) is convex for the Loewner order on the positive cone:

\[ (t a+(1-t) b)^p \le t\, a^p+(1-t)\, b^p. \]

This is the convex counterpart of the operator concavity of \(x\mapsto x^p\) on \([0,1]\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.37Complement of an orthogonal projection

If \(P \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is an orthogonal projection, then \(\mathbb {1}-P\) is an orthogonal projection.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.36Orthogonal projections and involutive-algebra projections

For a complex square matrix, the two descriptions \(P = P^\dagger \), \(P^2=P\) and \(P^* = P\), \(P^2=P\) are equivalent.

LaTeX
Lean
  • IsOrthogonalProjection.isStarProjection
  • IsStarProjection.isOrthogonalProjection
Theorem 6.5Orthogonal trace condition

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), and let \(A, B \ge 0\) be nonzero positive semidefinite matrices with \(\operatorname{tr}(B A) = 0\). Then there exists \(t\) with \(1 \le t \le D - 1\) such that

\[ \operatorname{tr}(B \cdot E^t(A)) {\gt} 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.25Overlap decay

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized tensors of the same bond dimension that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.30Overlap decay (irreducible-TP)

Let \(A, B\) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized tensors of the same bond dimension that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.23Rectangular overlap decay

If \(D_1 \neq D_2\) and both tensors are injective and normalized, then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.32Rectangular overlap decay (irreducible-TP)

If \(D_1 \neq D_2\) and both tensors are irreducible and trace-preserving normalized, then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.214Gap bound from cyclic overlap compression

Let \(A\) be a tensor and let \(L{\gt}1\). Suppose that, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair of cyclic length-\(L\) windows,

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \left(1-\frac{1}{4L}\right)\frac{1}{2(L-1)} \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then the parent Hamiltonian has the explicit gap constant

\[ \gamma _{L} := \frac{1}{4L} {\gt} 0. \]

Concretely, if \(N \ge 2L\) and \(v \in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^{\perp }\), then

\[ \gamma _{L} \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A) v\| . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.216Gap bound from a strict cyclic compression constant

Let \(L{\gt}1\). Put \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose that there is a constant \(\eta \ge 0\) such that \(\eta m{\lt}1\), and suppose that, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair of cyclic length-\(L\) windows,

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then the parent Hamiltonian has the gap constant \(1-\eta m{\gt}0\). Concretely, if \(N\ge 2L\) and \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \), then

\[ (1-\eta m)\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.217Gap bound from a cyclic operator-product norm constant

Let \(L{\gt}1\). Put \(m=2(L-1)\). Suppose there is a constant \(\eta \ge 0\) with \(\eta m{\lt}1\) and that, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair of cyclic length-\(L\) windows,

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\| , \]

that is, a bound \(\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\| \le \eta \) on the operator-product norm rather than the directional bound of Theorem 13.216. Then the parent Hamiltonian has the gap constant \(1-\eta m{\gt}0\). Concretely, if \(N\ge 2L\) and \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \), then

\[ (1-\eta m)\| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

This is a conditional reduction. The local terms \(h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}=p_i\) are the excitation projections. If their overlapping ranges have a common nonzero vector, then \(\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\| =1\), so the hypothesis is unsatisfiable for \(\eta {\lt}1\) in that case. In degenerate cases, such as a zero excitation projection, the operator-product norm can be smaller. In any case this is not the quantity controlled by the cited principal-angle estimates. The comparison is documented in docs/paper-gaps/cpgsv21_martingale_overlap.tex.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.6Overlap equals transfer trace

For tensors \(A, B\) of bond dimension \(D \ge 1\),

\[ O_{AB}(N) = \operatorname{Tr}(F_{AB}^N). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.7Rectangular overlap as transfer trace

For tensors \(A\) of bond dimension \(D_1 \ge 1\) and \(B\) of bond dimension \(D_2 \ge 1\),

\[ O_{AB}(N) = \operatorname{Tr}((F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}})^N). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.40Conditional BNT form after blocking for an arbitrary tensor

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor. Then there exists a blocking length \(p \ge 1\), nonzero weights \(\mu _k\), and blocks \(B_k\) over the blocked alphabet, such that:

  1. every block has positive bond dimension;

  2. every block is TP-normalized, primitive, irreducible, and injective;

  3. \(A^{[p]}\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family at every positive length.

Moreover, under \(|\mu _k| \le 1\) for every \(k\) and \(|\mu _k|=1\) for at least one \(k\), there exists a sector decomposition \(P\) over the blocked alphabet in the BNT canonical form of Definition 10.27 such that \(A^{[p]}\) and \(P\) generate the same MPV family at every positive length; the identity upgrades to all lengths once the total bond dimension of \(P\) equals that of \(A^{[p]}\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.exists_isBNTCanonicalForm_afterBlocking_pos
  • MPSTensor.exists_isBNTCanonicalForm_afterBlocking_of_totalDim
Theorem 10.35BNT from normal blocks with normalized weights

Let \(r \in \mathbb {N}\), and for each \(k = 1,\ldots ,r\) let \(D_k \in \mathbb {N}\), \(\mu _k \in \mathbb {C}\), and let \(B_k\) be an MPS tensor of physical dimension \(d\) and bond dimension \(D_k\). Assume:

  1. positive bond dimensions: \(D_k {\gt} 0\) for all \(k\),

  2. TP normalization: \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\),

  3. primitivity: \(\mathcal{E}_{B_k}\) is primitive for each \(k\),

  4. irreducibility: each \(B_k\) is irreducible,

  5. nonzero weights: \(\mu _k \neq 0\) for all \(k\),

  6. bounded weights: \(|\mu _k| \le 1\) for all \(k\),

  7. global unit witness: \(|\mu _k| = 1\) for some \(k\).

Then there exists a sector decomposition \(P\) such that \(P\) and \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family at every length, and \(P\) satisfies the basis-of-normal-tensors conditions (Definition 10.2) together with the multiplicity, span, and weight-bound conditions of Definition 10.27.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.170Kernel under the canonical \(\ell ^2\) identification

Under the canonical \(\ell ^2\) identification of the \(N\)-site state space, the parent ground space is exactly the kernel of the corresponding parent Hamiltonian.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.329Periodic-boundary containment in the BNT span

If \(A^i=\bigoplus _j\mu _j A_j^i\) is in block-injective canonical form, where \(A_1,\ldots ,A_g\) is a basis of normal tensors, assume \(g\ge 2\) and \(\mu _j\ne 0\) for every \(j\). Let \(L_0\ge 1\) be a common injectivity length for the blocks, and suppose

\[ L_0{\lt}L, \qquad L\ge 3(g-1)(L_0+1)+1, \qquad N\ge L+L_0, \]

and assume the block-diagonal boundary-condition comparison in the periodic ring,

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}\! \left(\bigoplus _j \mu _j A_j\right) \subseteq \bigvee _{j=1}^g\mathcal G_{N,L}(A_j). \]

then

\[ \mathcal G_{N,L}\! \left(\bigoplus _j \mu _j A_j\right) \subseteq \operatorname{span}\{ V^{(N)}(A_j): j=1,\ldots ,g\} . \]
LaTeX
Theorem 13.218Conditional spectral gap for MPS parent Hamiltonians

For a tensor \(A\) and a range \(L {\gt} 1\), assume either the cyclic-window anticommutator estimate of Theorem 13.215 or the stronger overlapping cyclic-window norm-compression estimate of Theorem 13.214. Then there exists \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) such that for every \(N \ge 2L\) and every vector \(v \in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^{\perp }\) one has

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A) v\| . \]

By Lemma 13.185, this implies the usual uniform lower bound on every nonzero eigenvalue of the parent Hamiltonian.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonian_gapped
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonian_gapped_of_anticommutator
Theorem 13.219Conditional spectral gap from a strict cyclic compression constant

For a tensor \(A\) and a range \(L{\gt}1\), suppose there is a constant \(\eta \ge 0\) such that \(\eta \, 2(L-1){\lt}1\) and, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair of cyclic length-\(L\) windows,

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then there exists \(\gamma {\gt}0\) such that, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every vector \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.220Conditional spectral gap from an operator-product norm constant

For a tensor \(A\) and a range \(L{\gt}1\), suppose there is a constant \(\eta \ge 0\) such that \(\eta \, 2(L-1){\lt}1\) and, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every overlapping off-diagonal pair of cyclic length-\(L\) windows,

\[ \| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}(h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v)\| \le \eta \, \| h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}v\| . \]

Then there exists \(\gamma {\gt}0\) such that, for every \(N\ge 2L\) and every vector \(v\in (\mathcal G_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A))^\perp \),

\[ \gamma \| v\| \le \| H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)v\| . \]

As in Theorem 13.217 this is a conditional reduction: the operator-product hypothesis is unsatisfiable for any overlapping pair whose excitation ranges have a common nonzero vector, since then \(\| h_{i,\mathrm{ES}}h_{j,\mathrm{ES}}\| =1\). The statement does not assert this norm identity in degenerate cases.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.182The finite-volume kernel is the cyclic constraint space

Assume \(N{\gt}0\) and \(L\le N\). Then

\[ \ker H_N(A,L)=G_L^{(N)}(A). \]

Equivalently, after the canonical Hilbert-space identification,

\[ \mathcal K_N^{\mathrm{ES}}(A,L) = e_N^{-1}G_L^{(N)}(A), \]

where \(\mathcal K_N^{\mathrm{ES}}(A,L)\) is the zero-energy space of \(H_{N,L}^{\mathrm{ES}}(A)\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.ker_parentHamiltonian_eq_chainGroundSpace
  • MPSTensor.parentHamiltonianGroundSpaceES_eq_chainGroundSpaceES
Theorem 13.179Zero energy gives the cyclic local constraints

Assume \(N{\gt}0\) and \(L\le N\). If a vector \(\psi \) has zero energy for the finite periodic parent Hamiltonian,

\[ H_N(A,L)\psi =0, \]

then every cyclic length-\(L\) restriction of \(\psi \) lies in the local MPS ground space. Equivalently,

\[ \ker H_N(A,L) \subseteq G_L^{(N)}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.14Basis-change law for the partial transpose

For matrices \(U,V\) on the first factor and a bipartite matrix \(\rho \), conjugating inside by \(V\) and \(U\), taking the first-factor partial transpose, and conjugating outside by \(U\) and \(V\) produces the original partial transpose conjugated by \(U U^{\mathsf T}\) and \(V^{\mathsf T}V\):

\[ \bigl(U\otimes \mathbb {1}\bigr) \Bigl[\bigl((V\otimes \mathbb {1})\, \rho \, (U\otimes \mathbb {1})\bigr)^{T_1}\Bigr] \bigl(V\otimes \mathbb {1}\bigr) =\bigl((U U^{\mathsf T})\otimes \mathbb {1}\bigr)\, \rho ^{T_1}\, \bigl((V^{\mathsf T}V)\otimes \mathbb {1}\bigr). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.10The partial transpose preserves Hermiticity

If \(\rho \) is Hermitian then so is its first-factor partial transpose \(\rho ^{T_1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.8The partial transpose is an involution

Applying the first-factor partial transpose twice returns the original matrix: \(\bigl(\rho ^{T_1}\bigr)^{T_1}=\rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.23The partial transpose distributes over a finite sum

For a finite family of bipartite matrices,

\[ \Bigl(\sum _i f_i\Bigr)^{T_1}=\sum _i f_i^{T_1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.11The two partial transposes differ by a global transposition

The second-factor partial transpose is the full transpose of the first-factor one:

\[ \rho ^{T_2}=\bigl(\rho ^{T_1}\bigr)^{\mathsf T}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.157Absorption by the left projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_{AX} \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.75Peripheral eigenvalues form a cyclic group

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital trace-preserving Schwarz map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. Then there exist \(m \ge 1\) and a primitive \(m\)-th root of unity \(\gamma \) such that \(m \mid D\) and the peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\) are exactly \(\{ 1, \gamma , \gamma ^2, \ldots , \gamma ^{m-1}\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.74Peripheral eigenvalues: cyclic structure

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Schwarz map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point (trace-preservation is not required). Then there exist \(m \ge 1\) and a primitive \(m\)-th root of unity \(\gamma \) such that the peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\) are exactly \(\{ 1, \gamma , \gamma ^2, \ldots , \gamma ^{m-1}\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.75Peripheral collapse under irreducibility at all times

If a quantum dynamical semigroup is irreducible at every positive time, then every peripheral eigenvalue of every positive-time slice equals \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.86Peripheral collapse at the reduced time step

Under the same hypotheses, every peripheral eigenvalue of \(T_u\) equals \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.52Finiteness of peripheral eigenvalues

On a finite-dimensional space, the set of peripheral eigenvalues is finite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.97Peripheral eigenvalue multiplicity one

Each peripheral eigenvalue has algebraic multiplicity one.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.72Peripheral eigenvectors become periodic fixed points

For every positive time \(t\), a quantum dynamical semigroup irreducible at every positive time has the following property: every peripheral eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(T_t\) admits a nonzero eigenvector \(V\) and a positive integer \(p\) such that \(T_t(V)=\mu V\) and \(T_{pt}(V)=V\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.54Peripheral powers imply root of unity

If the set of peripheral eigenvalues of a linear endomorphism \(E\) on a finite-dimensional space is closed under powers (\(\mu \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) and \(n \ge 1\) imply \(\mu ^n \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\)), then every peripheral eigenvalue is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.69Roots of unity via adjoint fixed point

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a Kraus map that is unital, and assume that the adjoint Kraus map \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) has a positive definite fixed point and that \(E\) is irreducible. Then every peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\) is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.53Power-stable peripheral eigenvalues are roots of unity

Let \(E\) be a linear endomorphism on a finite-dimensional space. If \(\mu \) is a peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\) and \(\mu ^n\) is an eigenvalue of \(E\) for every \(n \ge 1\), then \(\mu \) is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.73Peripheral eigenvectors with nonzero trace

For every positive time \(t\), under the same hypotheses every peripheral eigenvalue of \(T_t\) has a nonzero eigenvector with nonzero trace.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.16Perturbation bound

For \(t \ge 0\),

\[ \bigl\| e^{tL'} - e^{tL}\bigr\| \le t\, \| \Delta \| \cdot \sup _{s \in [0,t]}\| e^{sL}\| \cdot \sup _{s \in [0,t]}\| e^{sL'}\| , \]

where \(\Delta = L' - L\). This is [ Wol12 , Corollary 7.1 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.22PSD eigenvector for positive maps

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a positive linear map with \(D {\gt} 0\), and assume that \(E(\sigma ) \neq 0\) for every nonzero PSD matrix \(\sigma \). Then there exist a nonzero PSD matrix \(\rho \) and a positive real \(r {\gt} 0\) such that \(E(\rho ) = r \rho \).

This is the finite-dimensional Perron–Frobenius theorem for positive maps on matrix algebras ( [ Wol12 , Theorem 6.5 ] ; see also  [ EHK78 ] ). The proof applies Brouwer’s fixed-point theorem to the normalization map \(\rho \mapsto E(\rho ) / \operatorname{tr}(E(\rho ))\) on the compact convex set of density matrices.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.34Dual diagonalization for irreducible unital blocks

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor in unital gauge \(\sum _i A^i(A^i)^\dagger =\mathbb {1}\) with \(D{\gt}0\). Then there exist a unitary \(U\) and a diagonal positive definite \(\Lambda \) such that, for \(B^i:=U^\dagger A^iU\),

\begin{equation} \label{eq:canon_dual_diag_unital_pair} \sum _i B^i(B^i)^\dagger =\mathbb {1}, \qquad \sum _i (B^i)^\dagger \Lambda B^i=\Lambda , \end{equation}
2

and \(A\), \(B\) generate the same MPV family. This is the dual-map fixed-point and unitary-diagonalization step of [ PGVWC07 , Theorem 4 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.1Translation-invariant canonical form

Let \(A\) be a translation-invariant MPS representation of bond dimension \(D\). There is a positive scalar \(s\) and a finite family of blocks \(A_k\), possibly empty, such that the globally rescaled tensor \(s^{-1}A\) has the same MPV coefficients on every positive-length ring as

\[ \bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k^i. \]

The weights are positive and satisfy \(1\geq |\mu _k|\); if the family is nonempty, then some weight has norm \(1\). For each block,

\[ \sum _i A_k^i(A_k^i)^\dagger = \mathbb {1}, \qquad \sum _i (A_k^i)^\dagger \Lambda _k A_k^i = \Lambda _k, \]

where \(\Lambda _k\) is diagonal, positive, and full-rank. Each block has positive bond dimension. Moreover the only fixed points of \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}(X)=\sum _i A_k^iX(A_k^i)^\dagger \) are the scalar multiples of \(\mathbb {1}\): for every \(X\),

\[ \mathcal{E}_{A_k}(X)=X \Longrightarrow \exists \, c\in \mathbb {C}, X = c\, \mathbb {1}. \]

The total bond dimension of the decomposition is at most \(D\).

This is the positive-length form of [ PGVWC07 , Theorem 4 ] , with the global spectral-radius normalization step made explicit.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.160Cross-absorption by the left projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_K \circ P_{AX} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.158Absorption by the right projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_K \circ P_{XB} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.156Idempotence of the product projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_K \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.13Polarization of sesquilinear sandwiches

For any \(A, B, X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ 4\, A X B^\dagger = (A+B) X (A+B)^\dagger - (A-B) X (A-B)^\dagger + i\, (A + iB) X (A + iB)^\dagger - i\, (A - iB) X (A - iB)^\dagger . \]

Each of the four summands on the right is a single-Kraus CP map, so every sesquilinear sandwich decomposes into a signed complex linear combination of CP maps.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.24Positive-definite adjoint eigenvector for irreducible tensors

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\) and some \(A^i \neq 0\). Then there exist a positive definite matrix \(\sigma \) and a positive real \(r {\gt} 0\) such that \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\sigma ) = r \sigma \).

This combines the general PF existence ( [ Wol12 , Theorem 6.5 ] ) with the irreducible upgrade to positive definiteness ( [ Wol12 , Theorem 6.3(2) ] ); the application to the adjoint transfer map follows  [ CPGSV16 , Appendix A ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.104Positive maps preserve adjoints

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive, then \(T(A^\dagger ) = T(A)^\dagger \) for all \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.105Order intervals are preserved

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be positive with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\). If \(a \le 0 \le b\) and \(a \mathbb {1}\le A \le b \mathbb {1}\), then

\[ a \mathbb {1}\le T(A) \le b \mathbb {1}. \]

This is the matrix form of [ Wol12 , Equation (5.21) ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.103Positive maps are monotone

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive and \(A \le B\), then \(T(A) \le T(B)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.28Positive maps preserve Hermiticity

If \(E\) is positive and \(X = X^\dagger \), then \(E(X) = E(X)^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.106Spectral interval contractivity

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be positive with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\), and let \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be Hermitian. If \(\operatorname{spec}(A) \subseteq [a,b]\) with \(a \le 0 \le b\), then

\[ \operatorname{spec}(T(A)) \subseteq [a,b]. \]

This is [ Wol12 , Equation (5.21) ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.18Jensen for concave log

For a positive unital map \(T\) and positive-definite \(A\):

\[ T(\log A) \le \log (TA). \]

This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.13 ] for \(f=\log \). Requires unitality (\(T(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\)), not merely subunitality.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.14Jensen for concave real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\), a positive semidefinite matrix \(A\), and \(p\in [0,1]\):

\[ T(A^p) \le (TA)^p. \]

This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.13 ] for concave \(f(x)=x^p\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.16Jensen for convex real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\), a positive semidefinite matrix \(A\), and \(p\in [1,2]\):

\[ (TA)^p \le T(A^p). \]

This is [ Wol12 , Theorem 5.11 ] for the subunital operator-convex case \(f(x)=x^p\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.47Power decomposition

If \(E\) is trace-preserving and \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then for \(n \ge 1\),

\[ E^n = P + (E - P)^n, \]

where \(P\) is the fixed-point projection.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.21Equal power sums imply equal multisets

Let \(\alpha , \beta : \{ 0, \ldots , n-1\} \to \mathbb {C}\). If \(\sum _i \alpha _i^k = \sum _i \beta _i^k\) for all \(k \ge 1\), then the multisets \(\{ \alpha _i\} \) and \(\{ \beta _i\} \) are equal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.34Prepared BNT blocks after blocking

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor. Then there is a blocking length \(p\ge 1\), nonzero weights \(\mu _k\), and blocks \(B_k\) over the \(p\)-blocked alphabet such that:

  1. each block has positive bond dimension;

  2. each block is trace-preserving, primitive, tensor-irreducible, and one-site injective;

  3. \(A^{[p]}\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family at every positive length.

No weight-modulus normalization is asserted at this stage.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.60Primitive overlap convergence

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then \(O_{AA}(N) \to 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.59Adjoint primitivity equivalence

Let \(\mathcal{E}\) be a finite-dimensional completely positive map. Then \(\mathcal{E}\) is primitive if and only if its Frobenius adjoint \(\mathcal{E}^\dagger \) is primitive; equivalently, \(\sigma _\partial (\mathcal{E}) = \{ 1\} \) iff \(\sigma _\partial (\mathcal{E}^\dagger ) = \{ 1\} \), since the peripheral spectrum of \(\mathcal{E}^\dagger \) is the complex conjugate of that of \(\mathcal{E}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.60Primitivity under blocking by a multiple

Let \(A\) have positive bond dimension and let \(p_0,p\ge 1\) with \(p_0 \mid p\). If \(\mathcal{E}_{A^{[p_0]}}\) is primitive, then \(\mathcal{E}_{A^{[p]}}\) is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.69Primitive powers annihilate trace-zero matrices

If \(E\) is an irreducible primitive channel with faithful fixed density \(\sigma \), then \(E^n(X) \to 0\) for every matrix \(X\) with \(\operatorname{tr}(X)=0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.75Complementary transfer-map gap for primitive maps

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be trace-preserving, and let \(\rho \neq 0\) satisfy \(E(\rho ) = \rho \) and \(\operatorname{tr}(\rho ) \neq 0\). Let

\[ P(X) = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )} \rho \]

be the fixed-point projection associated to \(\rho \). Assume that:

  1. \(E\) is primitive;

  2. every eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(E\) satisfies \(|\mu | \le 1\);

  3. whenever \(E(X) = X\) and \(\operatorname{tr}(X) = 0\), one has \(X = 0\).

Then every eigenvalue \(\nu \) of \(E - P\) satisfies \(|\nu | {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.73Primitivity equals period one

A linear map with a nonzero fixed point is primitive if and only if its period is \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.87Primitivity at the reduced time step

Under the same hypotheses, \(T_u\) is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.76Primitivity from irreducibility at all times

If a quantum dynamical semigroup is irreducible at every positive time, then every positive-time slice is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.99Self-overlap convergence from a complementary transfer-map gap

Let \(A\) be a normalized MPS tensor with a nonzero PSD fixed point \(\rho \) of the transfer map. If \(\rho _{\operatorname{spec}}(\mathcal{E}_A - P) {\lt} 1\) (where \(P\) is the fixed-point projection), then \(O_{AA}(N) \to 1\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.25Projective equivalence and cohomologous cocycles

Let \(\rho _1,\rho _2\) be projective representations with factor systems \(\omega _1,\omega _2\). Then

\[ \rho _1 \sim _{\mathrm{proj}} \rho _2 \iff \omega _1 \sim \omega _2. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.10Existence of PSD fixed point

Assume \(D \ge 1\). Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor with \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) (so that \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is trace-preserving). Then there exists \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\), with \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.3PSD fixed point is positive definite

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor and let \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\), be a fixed point of the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \). Then \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.4PSD fixed point is positive definite — irreducible version

If \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible and \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\) is a fixed point, then \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.6Uniqueness of PSD fixed point

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor. If \(\rho , \sigma \ge 0\) are both nonzero fixed points of \(\mathcal{E}_A\), then \(\sigma = c \rho \) for some \(c \in \mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.8Irreducible fixed-point uniqueness

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor whose transfer map is irreducible. If \(\rho ,\sigma \ge 0\) are fixed points of \(\mathcal{E}_A\), and if \(\rho \) is nonzero, then \(\sigma =c\rho \) for some scalar \(c\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.56Self-duality of the positive semidefinite cone

Let \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be Hermitian. If \(\operatorname {tr}(AB)\geq 0\) for every positive semidefinite matrix \(B\), then \(A\geq 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.14Forward Lorentz-cone trace inequality

If \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive semidefinite, then

\[ \operatorname {Re}\operatorname{tr}(A^2) \le \left(\operatorname {Re}\operatorname{tr}(A)\right)^2 . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.19Hughston–Jozsa–Wootters equivalence

Under the cardinality hypothesis \(|\iota _2| \le |\iota _1|\), two pure-state ensembles induce the same density operator iff they are related by a tall isometric mixing matrix.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.17Isometric mixing preserves the density

If two pure-state ensembles \(\{ \psi _i\} _{i \in \iota _1}\) and \(\{ \phi _j\} _{j \in \iota _2}\) are related by an isometric mixing matrix \(V \in \mathbb {C}^{\iota _1 \times \iota _2}\) with \(V^\dagger V = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\psi _i = \sum _j V_{ij} \phi _j\), then they induce the same pure-ensemble density operator. This is the sufficient direction of the Hughston–Jozsa–Wootters theorem; the converse is Theorem 16.18.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.159Cross-absorption by the right projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_{XB} \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.94Irreducibility iff primitivity for a quantum dynamical semigroup

For a quantum dynamical semigroup \(T_t = e^{tL}\):

\[ (\exists \, t_0 {\gt} 0,\ T_{t_0}\ \text{irreducible}) \iff (\forall \, t {\gt} 0,\ T_t\ \text{primitive and irreducible}). \]

This is [ Wol12 , Proposition 7.5 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.11Quantum Perron–Frobenius

Assume \(D \ge 1\). Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor with \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\). Then the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\) has a unique PSD fixed point \(\rho \) (up to scaling), and \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.12Quantum Perron–Frobenius for irreducible transfer maps

Assume \(D\ge 1\). Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor such that its transfer map is irreducible and \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i=\mathbb {1}\) (so that \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is trace-preserving). Then \(\mathcal{E}_A\) has a unique positive definite fixed point, up to scalar multiple.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.16Relative entropy in entropy form

If \(\rho \) is Hermitian, then

\[ D(\rho \Vert \sigma ) = -S(\rho )-\operatorname {Re}\operatorname {tr}(\rho \log \sigma ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.52Rank-\(k\) projection form of the Choi criterion

Assume \(D{\gt}0\) and \(k\le D\). A linear map \(T:M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) is \(k\)-positive if and only if, for every Hermitian projection \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) of rank \(k\),

\[ R_P\tau R_P^\dagger \geq 0, \]

where \(\tau \) is the Choi matrix of \(T\). This is the rank-\(k\) projection formulation of [ Wol12 , Chapter 3, Proposition 3.1 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.51Projection-compression converse for the Choi criterion

Assume \(D{\gt}0\) and \(k\le D\). Let \(T:M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) have Choi matrix \(\tau \). Suppose that

\[ R_P\tau R_P^\dagger \geq 0 \]

for every Hermitian projection \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) of rank \(k\). Then \(T\) is \(k\)-positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.50Rank-\(k\) right projections in the Choi criterion

Assume \(D{\gt}0\). Let \(T:M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) be \(k\)-positive, with Choi matrix \(\tau \). If \(P\in M_{D,D}(\mathbb {C})(\mathbb {C})\) is Hermitian, satisfies \(P^2=P\), and has rank \(k\), then

\[ R_P\tau R_P^\dagger \geq 0. \]

This is the forward rank-\(k\) projection-compression direction of [ Wol12 , Chapter 3, Proposition 3.1 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.56Rank-one extraction for blocked tensor

Suppose \(A\) is normal and \(N_0 \ge 1\) with \(S_{N_0}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). Then there exist words \(\sigma _0, \tau _0\) of length \(N_0\), nonzero vectors \(\varphi , \psi \), nonzero scalars \(\mu , \nu \), and \(m \in \mathbb {N}\) such that:

  1. \(A^{\sigma _0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \),

  2. \((A^{\tau _0})^T \psi = \nu \psi \),

  3. \(\varphi \psi ^T \in S_m(A^{[N_0]})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.21Rectangular intertwining forces equal bond dimension

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be injective tensors of bond dimensions \(D_1\) and \(D_2\). If there exist \(X \in M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C}) \setminus \{ 0\} \) and \(\mu \in \mathbb {C}\) such that, for every physical index \(i\),

\[ X (B^i)^\dagger = \mu (A^i)^\dagger X, \qquad A^i X = \mu X B^i, \]

then \(D_1 = D_2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.53The two boundary-crossing compatibilities

Let \(A\) be \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0{\gt}0\) and \(D \ge 1\). Suppose \(\psi \in \mathcal G_{N,L}(A)\), \(N \ge 2\), \(L_0 {\lt} L \le N\), and \(\psi = \Gamma _N(X)\) for a boundary matrix \(X\). Then there are two families of boundary matrices \(Y^+_\tau \) and \(Y^-_\tau \), indexed by fixed complement values \(\tau \), such that for every physical letter \(j\),

\begin{equation} \label{eq:ph_reduced_wrapped_compat} C^+_\tau A^j X = Y^+_\tau A^j, \qquad X A^j C^-_\tau = A^j Y^-_\tau , \end{equation}
8

where \(C^+_\tau \) and \(C^-_\tau \) are the complementary words exposed by the two boundary-crossing cyclic intervals, both of reduced length \(L_0+1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.5Operator-implication step of the reduction criterion

Let \(n\ge 1\) and let \(\rho \) be a bipartite matrix, with \(\rho ^{F}\) its image under the factor swap \(\rho ^{F}_{(i_2,i_1),(j_2,j_1)}=\rho _{(i_1,i_2),(j_1,j_2)}\). If \((T_n\otimes \operatorname{id})(\rho )\ge 0\) then

\[ n\, \mathbb {1}\otimes \rho _2\ge \rho , \]

and if \((T_n\otimes \operatorname{id})(\rho ^{F})\ge 0\) then

\[ n\, \rho _1\otimes \mathbb {1}\ge \rho , \]

where \(\rho _1\) and \(\rho _2\) are the reduced densities of \(\rho \) on the first and second factors. Because the factor swap is a unitary reindexing,

\[ (\operatorname{id}\otimes T_n)(\rho )\ge 0 \iff (T_n\otimes \operatorname{id})(\rho ^{F})\ge 0, \]

so the second hypothesis is Wolf’s symmetric condition \((\operatorname{id}\otimes T_n)(\rho )\ge 0\) expressed through the swap.

This is the second step of Wolf’s two-step argument for [ Wol12 , Equation (3.18) ] . The source states the inequalities under the premise that \(\rho \) has Schmidt number at most \(n\), and reaches \((T_n\otimes \operatorname{id})(\rho )\ge 0\) from that premise by the \(n\)-positivity of \(T_n\); that first step is taken here as the hypothesis rather than derived. The first step and the composed full criterion with the Schmidt-number premise are Theorem 20.35.

LaTeX
Lean
  • Matrix.reductionCriterion_left
  • Matrix.reductionCriterion_right
Theorem 20.35Reduction criterion with the Schmidt-number premise

For \(1\le n{\lt}D\), a bipartite state on the square system \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\otimes M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) of Schmidt number at most \(n\) satisfies the reduction criterion

\[ n\, \rho _1\otimes \mathbb {1}\ge \rho \qquad \text{and}\qquad n\, \mathbb {1}\otimes \rho _2\ge \rho , \]

with \(\rho _1\) and \(\rho _2\) the reduced densities on the first and second factors.

LaTeX
Lean
  • Matrix.reductionCriterion_left_of_hasSchmidtNumberLE
  • Matrix.reductionCriterion_right_of_hasSchmidtNumberLE
Theorem 20.1The reduction map is the family \(T_\eta \) at integer parameter

For every \(k\in \mathbb {N}\) the reduction map \(T_k\) equals the family map \(T_\eta \) at \(\eta =k\):

\[ T_k=T_\eta \big|_{\eta =k}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.7The first reduction map is positive

For \(D\ge 1\), the map \(T_1(X)=\operatorname{tr}(X)\mathbb {1}-X\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.8Self-duality of the reduction map

The reduction map is self-dual for the trace pairing:

\[ \operatorname{tr}(T_k(\rho )X)=\operatorname{tr}(\rho T_k(X)). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.25Ancilla additivity of the quantum relative entropy

For positive definite matrices \(\rho , \sigma \) and a positive definite matrix \(\tau \) of unit trace,

\[ D(\rho \otimes \tau \, \Vert \, \sigma \otimes \tau ) = D(\rho \Vert \sigma ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.30Data-processing inequality under the partial trace

For positive definite matrices \(\rho , \sigma \) on a tensor product of a system factor and an ancilla factor,

\[ D(\operatorname{tr}_C \rho \, \Vert \, \operatorname{tr}_C \sigma ) \le D(\rho \, \Vert \, \sigma ), \]

where \(\operatorname{tr}_C\) is the partial trace over the ancilla factor. (Scope restriction: the source inequality holds for density operators with \(\ker \sigma \subseteq \ker \rho \); here both arguments are positive definite. Recorded in the relative-entropy elimination route note for strong subadditivity.)

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.19Joint convexity of the relative entropy

On pairs of positive definite matrices in \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the map \((\rho ,\sigma )\mapsto D(\rho \Vert \sigma )\) is jointly convex.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.20Joint convexity on the support domain

On pairs of density matrices \((\rho ,\sigma )\) in \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying the support condition \(\ker \sigma \subseteq \ker \rho \), the map \((\rho ,\sigma )\mapsto D(\rho \Vert \sigma )\) is jointly convex.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.23Unitary invariance of the quantum relative entropy

For Hermitian matrices \(\rho , \sigma \) and a unitary \(U\),

\[ D(U\rho U^\dagger \, \Vert \, U\sigma U^\dagger ) = D(\rho \Vert \sigma ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.15Renormalization-fixed-point characterization

An MPS tensor \(A\) is a renormalization fixed point if and only if there exists an isometry \(V : \mathbb {C}^d \to \mathbb {C}^{d^2}\), written as coefficients \(V_{(i_1,i_2),j}\) with \(V^\dagger V = 1\), such that

\begin{equation} \label{eq:corr_AA_isometry} A^{i_1} A^{i_2} = \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} V_{(i_1,i_2),j} A^j \end{equation}
2

for all \(i_1,i_2 \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d-1\} \). This is [ CPGSV16 , Theorem 3.1 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.83Residual slice decomposition

For \(u \ge 0\) and \(s {\gt} 0\), one has \(r_n \in [0,s]\) and

\[ nu = m_n s + r_n. \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • residualSliceTime_mem_Icc
  • residualSlice_decomp
Theorem 13.161Reverse product identity

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is idempotent-product data, then

\[ P_{XB} \circ P_{AX} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.31Canonical-form blocks are injective

For a family of tensors in canonical form, every block is injective. This is a direct projection of the injectivity field of the canonical-form predicate; see [ CPGSV16 , Section 2.3 ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.38Single-block RFP iff ZCL inside a canonical form

Let \(A^i=\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k^i\) be a tensor in canonical form. For each \(k\), the tensor \(A_k\) is a renormalization fixed point if and only if \(A_k\) has zero correlation length.

This is the single-block consequence of [ CPGSV16 , Theorem 3.10 ] . The ZCL condition in the source theorem is the BNT-family condition: CID together with the local orthogonality equations

\[ \mathbb E_{j,j'}=0 \qquad (j\ne j'), \]

as in Definition 14.18.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.146Renormalization fixed points have commuting nearest-neighbor parent terms

If \(A\) has nonzero virtual dimension and is a normal renormalization fixed point, then for every chain length \(N \ge 2\) the two-site parent terms commute. This theorem records the commutation part of the nearest-neighbor source condition, not the ground-space spanning part. This implication is cited here from the structural characterization theorem for renormalization fixed points in  [ CPGSV16 ] ; the source proof says that RFP \(\Rightarrow \) NNCPH is immediate from that theorem. At present this implication remains an unproved hypothesis. The source derivation from the basic-vector formula \(V^{(N)}(A_j)=U^{\otimes N}\varphi _j^{\otimes N}\) and the corresponding two-site projectors remains to be proved.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.rfp_implies_nncph
  • Axioms.rfp_to_nncph_commute
Theorem 13.147Renormalization fixed points satisfy the NNCPH commutation and zero-energy equations

If \(A\) has nonzero virtual dimension and is a normal renormalization fixed point, then for every chain length \(N \ge 2\) the MPS vector \(V^{(N)}(A)\) satisfies the commutation and zero-energy equations for nearest-neighbor parent terms:

\[ h_i h_j = h_j h_i,\qquad h_i V^{(N)}(A)=0. \]

The zero-energy equation is the usual parent-Hamiltonian frustration-freeness statement. The commuting part is still inherited from the axiom tagged above, so this entry is not yet the full source implication.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.rfp_implies_nncph_ground_state
  • Axioms.rfp_to_nncph_commute
Theorem 14.21Unitary diagonal fixed point for a left-canonical normal RFP

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). Let \(A\) be a normal renormalization fixed point such that

\[ \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}. \]

Then there exist a unitary \(U\) and a diagonal positive definite matrix \(\Lambda \) such that, for \(B^i := U^\dagger A^i U\),

\[ \sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\qquad \text{and}\qquad \mathcal{E}_B(\Lambda ) = \Lambda . \]

This is the diagonal fixed-point reduction in [ CPGSV16 , Appendix B ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.19RFP normal tensor is injective

If \(A\) is a nonzero normal RFP tensor, then \(A\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.23Full structural form for RFP tensors

A normal tensor \(A\) in canonical form II that is a renormalization fixed point admits a decomposition \(A^i = X \Lambda U^i X^{-1}\), where \(\Lambda \) is diagonal positive, \(\sum _i (U^i)^\dagger U^i=I\), and

\[ \sum _i \overline{(U^i)_{\alpha ,\beta }}\, (U^i)_{\alpha ',\beta '} = D^{-1}\delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.26Per-block isometry canonical form

When each block \(A_k\) of a multi-block tensor is a normal, left-canonical renormalization fixed point, that block admits the isometry decomposition \(A_k^i = X_k \Lambda _k U_k^i X_k^{-1}\), with \(X_k\) invertible, \(\Lambda _k\) diagonal positive, and \(U_k = (U_k^i)\) satisfies \(\sum _i (U_k^i)^\dagger U_k^i = I\) and the pair-index orthonormality condition

\[ \sum _i \overline{(U_k^i)_{\alpha ,\beta }}\, (U_k^i)_{\alpha ',\beta '} = D_k^{-1}\delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '} . \]

This is the blockwise form of the isometry canonical form (Theorem 14.23); see [ CPGSV16 , Section 3 ] . The source additionally imposes the normalization \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda _k) = 1\); the statement here gives positive \(\Lambda _k\) without it. That normalization is genuine rather than a conjugation gauge, since rescaling \(\Lambda _k \mapsto \Lambda _k/\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda _k)\) factors out as an overall scalar on \(A_k^i\) (conjugation by \(X_k\) preserves the scale); the statement is thus the un-normalized isometry form. Scope restriction (source isometry). Corollary III.cor3 also invokes the joint isometry condition

\[ \sum _i (U_k^i)_{\alpha ,\beta }\, \overline{(U_\ell ^i)_{\alpha ',\beta '}} = \delta _{k,\ell }\delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '}. \]

The theorem above records the contracted per-block condition \(\sum _i (U_k^i)^\dagger U_k^i=I\) and the diagonal pair-index equation with right-hand side \(D_k^{-1}\delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '}\). It does not impose the trace-normalization \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda _k)=1\), and it does not include the cross-block equations for \(k\ne \ell \). This restriction is recorded in docs/paper-gaps/cpsv16_rfp_isometry_scope.tex.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.27Per-block unit pair-index form

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 14.26, each block admits a decomposition \(A_k^i = X_k \Lambda _k U_k^i X_k^{-1}\) with \(\Lambda _k\) diagonal positive and

\[ \sum _i \overline{(U_k^i)_{\alpha ,\beta }}\, (U_k^i)_{\alpha ',\beta '} = \delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '} . \]

This is a blockwise unit pair-index comparison. It still does not impose the source trace-normalization of \(\Lambda _k\) or the cross-block orthogonality equations for distinct blocks.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.24Structural form with diagonal square-sum identity

The same decomposition as the full structural form (Theorem 14.23) additionally records that the diagonal weights satisfy \(\sum _\alpha \Lambda _\alpha ^2 = D\). This square-sum identity is the trace-normalization seed: the explicit weights are \(\Lambda _\alpha = \sqrt{D\, \rho _{\alpha ,\alpha }/\operatorname{tr}\rho }\), and \(\sum _\alpha \rho _{\alpha ,\alpha } = \operatorname{tr}\rho \) for the diagonal fixed point \(\rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.25Unit pair-index form for RFP tensors

A normal tensor \(A\) in canonical form II that is a renormalization fixed point admits a decomposition \(A^i = X \Lambda U^i X^{-1}\), where \(\Lambda \) is diagonal positive and

\[ \sum _i \overline{(U^i)_{\alpha ,\beta }}\, (U^i)_{\alpha ',\beta '} = \delta _{\alpha ,\alpha '}\delta _{\beta ,\beta '} . \]

This is the unit pair-index convention of [ CPGSV16 , Section 3 ] . The statement still does not impose the trace-normalization \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.20Left-canonical normal RFP tensor is injective

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). Let \(A\) be a normal renormalization fixed point such that

\[ \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}. \]

Then \(A\) is injective. This is the left-canonical injectivity step in [ CPGSV16 , Appendix B ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.32RG flow convergence for canonical-form tensors

For a tensor in canonical form with blocks \((A_k)\) and weights \((\mu _k)\), the iterated blocking \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}^{2^n}\) converges pointwise to an idempotent linear map \(\mathcal{E}_\infty \) as \(n\to \infty \). That is, there exists \(\mathcal{E}_\infty \) with \(\mathcal{E}_\infty ^2 = \mathcal{E}_\infty \) such that \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}^{2^n}(\rho ) \to \mathcal{E}_\infty (\rho )\) for all density matrices \(\rho \). This is [ CPGSV16 , Appendix B ] .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.13Right-canonical (unital) gauge

Let \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \) and let \(S\) be an invertible matrix with \(S S^\dagger = \rho \). Define the gauged operators \(A'^i = S^{-1} A^i S\). Then \(\sum _i A'^i (A'^i)^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), i.e. the gauged Kraus map is unital. This is the right-canonical normalization.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.44Inverting step for word compatibility

Assume \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0 {\gt} 0\). If matrices \((Z_j)_j\) satisfy

\[ Z_j A^\sigma = A^j Y_\sigma \]

for every word \(\sigma \) of length \(K\), then there exists a matrix \(X\) such that \(Z_j = A^j X\) for all \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.90Right multiplicative identity from KS equality

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map. If \(E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X)\), then \(E(YX) = E(Y)E(X)\) for all \(Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.15Map form of Jensen for concave real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\), a positive semidefinite matrix \(A\), and \(p\in [0,1]\), the inequality \(T(A^p) \le (TA)^p\) holds.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.17Map form of Jensen for convex real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\), a positive semidefinite matrix \(A\), and \(p\in [1,2]\), the inequality \((TA)^p \le T(A^p)\) holds.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.62Positive-length equality survives blocking

If two tensors have the same MPV coefficients at every positive length, then so do their blocked tensors after any positive blocking length.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.63Positive-length blocking of a weighted block sum

If \(A\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) have the same MPV coefficients at every positive length, then after any positive blocking length \(p\), \(A^{[p]}\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k^{\, p} B_k^{[p]}\) have the same MPV coefficients at every positive length.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.64Positive-length equality of powered block sums

If \(\bigoplus _j \mu ^A_j A_j\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu ^B_k B_k\) have the same MPV coefficients at every positive length, then after any positive common blocking length \(p\), \(\bigoplus _j (\mu ^A_j)^p A_j^{[p]}\) and \(\bigoplus _k (\mu ^B_k)^p B_k^{[p]}\) have the same MPV coefficients at every positive length.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.8Unitary conjugation preserves the MPV family

If \(U\) is a unitary matrix, then \(A\) and \(U^\dagger A U\) generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.40Absorbs the adjoint map

If \(T(\sigma )=\sigma \), then \(E_\sigma \circ T^* = E_\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.37Idempotence

\(E_\sigma (E_\sigma (X)) = E_\sigma (X)\) for all \(X\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.43Scalar map is a conditional expectation

Let \(K\) be trace-preserving, let \(\rho {\gt}0\) be a positive-definite fixed point of the associated channel \(T(\rho )=\sum _i K_i\rho K_i^\dagger \) (so \(T(\rho )=\rho \)), and assume every adjoint fixed point of \(T^*\) is a scalar multiple of \(\mathbb {1}\). Then \(E_\rho \) is a conditional expectation onto the adjoint fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.44Complete positivity of the scalar conditional expectation

For any positive semidefinite \(\sigma \), the scalar conditional expectation \(E_\sigma \) is a completely positive map.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.45\(\sigma \)-trace preservation of the scalar conditional expectation

If \(\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )\neq 0\), then for every \(X\)

\[ \operatorname{tr}(\sigma \, E_\sigma (X)) = \operatorname{tr}(\sigma X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.36Unitality

\(E_\sigma (\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.12Basic bounds for Schmidt rank

The zero vector has Schmidt rank zero, every vector satisfies

\[ \operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le \min (D,k), \]

and every product vector \(u\otimes v\) has Schmidt rank at most one.

LaTeX

  • Matrix.schmidtRank_le_left
  • Matrix.schmidtRank_le_right
  • Matrix.schmidtRank_le_min
  • Matrix.schmidtRank_product_le_one
  • Matrix.hasSchmidtRankLE_one_product
Theorem 5.38Converse Schmidt-rank Choi test

Assume \(D{\gt}0\). If

\[ \langle \psi ,\tau _T\psi \rangle \ge 0 \]

for every \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^D\) with \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le k\), then \(T\) is \(k\)-positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.39Schmidt-rank Choi criterion

Assume \(D{\gt}0\). Then \(T\) is \(k\)-positive if and only if

\[ \langle \psi ,\tau _T\psi \rangle \ge 0 \]

for every \(\psi \in \mathbb {C}^D\otimes \mathbb {C}^D\) with \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.14Schmidt singular values and Schmidt rank

The Schmidt singular values are indexed from zero. The nonzero Schmidt singular values of \(\psi \) are exactly those with index below \(\operatorname {SR}(\psi )\). Equivalently, for zero-based indexing,

\[ s_k(\psi )=0 \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad \operatorname {SR}(\psi )\le k . \]
LaTeX
Lean
